    if not (f.layout and f.layout.mode == "abstract_stencil") then err(errors, label .. " non-executable stencil missing abstract layout") end
    if r.code_offset == 0 and r.code_offset_kind ~= "abstract_zero" then err(errors, label .. " zero placeholder code_offset must be marked abstract_zero") end
  end
end

function M.validate_stencil(f)
  local errors = {}
  if type(f) ~= "table" then return false, { "missing stencil" } end

  if f.schema ~= "spon.stencil.abstract.v1" and f.schema ~= "spon.stencil.native.v1" then err(errors, "unknown stencil schema: " .. tostring(f.schema)) end
  if not has_kind(M.PHYSICAL_ABI, f.physical_abi) then err(errors, "unknown physical ABI: " .. tostring(f.physical_abi)) end
  if f.executable then
    if not (f.layout and f.layout.mode == "native_bytes" and f.layout.executable == true) then
      err(errors, "native stencil must declare executable native byte layout")
    end
    if type(f.code_hex) ~= "string" or #f.code_hex == 0 then err(errors, "native stencil missing code_hex") end
    if tonumber(f.code_size or 0) <= 0 then err(errors, "native stencil missing positive code_size") end
  else
    if not (f.layout and f.layout.mode == "abstract_stencil" and f.layout.executable == false) then
      err(errors, "abstract stencil must declare non-executable abstract layout")
    end
  end
  if #(f.clobbers or {}) == 0 then err(errors, "stencil must declare clobbers") end

  local entry_count, ok_count = 0, 0
  for i, ep in ipairs(f.endpoints or {}) do
    if not has_kind(M.ENDPOINT_KIND, ep.kind) then err(errors, "unknown endpoint kind at " .. i .. ": " .. tostring(ep.kind)) end
    if #(ep.locations or {}) == 0 then err(errors, "endpoint without location contract at " .. i .. ": " .. tostring(ep.kind)) end
    for j, loc in ipairs(ep.locations or {}) do validate_location(loc, errors, "endpoint " .. i .. " location " .. j) end
    if ep.kind == "entry" then entry_count = entry_count + 1 end
    if ep.kind == "ok" then ok_count = ok_count + 1 end
    local nproj = tonumber(ep.n_projections or 0) or 0
    if ep.kind ~= "entry" and ep.kind ~= "ok" then
      if nproj <= 0 then
        err(errors, "non-success endpoint without projection: " .. tostring(ep.kind))
      else
        local start = tonumber(ep.projection_start)
        if not start or start < 1 then
          err(errors, "endpoint projection range missing start at " .. i)
        elseif start + nproj - 1 > #(f.projections or {}) then
          err(errors, "endpoint projection range out of bounds at " .. i)
        else
          for k = start, start + nproj - 1 do validate_projection_object(f.projections[k], errors, "projection " .. k) end
        end
        if #(ep.projections or {}) > 0 then
          if #ep.projections ~= nproj then err(errors, "endpoint embedded projection count mismatch at " .. i) end
          for j, p in ipairs(ep.projections or {}) do validate_projection_object(p, errors, "endpoint " .. i .. " projection " .. j) end
        end
      end
    elseif nproj ~= 0 then
      err(errors, "success/entry endpoint must not carry projections at " .. i)
    end
  end
  if entry_count ~= 1 then err(errors, "stencil requires exactly one entry endpoint") end
  if ok_count < 1 then err(errors, "stencil requires at least one ok endpoint") end

  local ft = f.fact_transfer
  if type(ft) ~= "table" then
    err(errors, "missing fact_transfer")
  else
    for _, k in ipairs({"selector_sig", "required_sig", "checked_sig", "produced_sig", "killed_sig"}) do
      if ft[k] == nil then err(errors, "missing fact_transfer." .. k) end
    end
  end

  for i, h in ipairs(f.data_relocs or {}) do
    validate_abstract_offset(f, h, errors, "data reloc " .. i)
    local role = h.role_kind or h.reloc_kind
    if DATA_WORDS_AS_CONTROL[role] then err(errors, "control role used as data reloc at " .. i .. ": " .. tostring(role)) end
    if not has_kind(DATA_ROLE, role) then err(errors, "unknown data reloc role at " .. i .. ": " .. tostring(role)) end
    if not has_kind(M.RELOC_KIND, h.reloc_kind or "unknown") then err(errors, "unknown data reloc encoding at " .. i .. ": " .. tostring(h.reloc_kind)) end
    if PAYLOAD_ROLE[role] and not f.allow_payload_roles then err(errors, "unsupported payload data reloc role: " .. tostring(role)) end
  end

  for i, cr in ipairs(f.control_relocs or {}) do
    validate_abstract_offset(f, cr, errors, "control reloc " .. i)
    local edge = cr.edge_kind or cr.reloc_kind
    if CONTROL_WORDS_AS_DATA[edge] then err(errors, "data role used as control reloc at " .. i .. ": " .. tostring(edge)) end
    if not has_kind(M.CONTROL_RELOC_KIND, edge) then err(errors, "unknown control reloc kind at " .. i .. ": " .. tostring(edge)) end
    if not has_kind(M.RELOC_KIND, cr.reloc_kind or "unknown") then err(errors, "unknown control reloc encoding at " .. i .. ": " .. tostring(cr.reloc_kind)) end
    if cr.endpoint_index == nil then err(errors, "control reloc without endpoint index at " .. i) end
    if cr.endpoint_index ~= nil and not (f.endpoints or {})[cr.endpoint_index] then err(errors, "control reloc endpoint out of range at " .. i) end
  end

  for i, p in ipairs(f.projections or {}) do validate_projection_object(p, errors, "projection " .. i) end

  if #(f.dependencies or {}) > 0 and not f.allow_dependencies then err(errors, "unsupported dependencies in native stencil descriptor") end

  return #errors == 0, errors
end

local function loc_to_abi(loc)
  return {
    kind = M.LOCATION_KIND_ID[loc.kind or "none"] or 0,
    value_type = M.VALUE_TYPE_ID[loc.value_type or "Unknown"] or 0,
    reg = REG_ID[loc.reg or "none"] or 0,
    index = loc.index or 0,
    name = loc.name,
  }
end

local function proj_to_abi(p)
  return {
    kind = M.PROJECTION_KIND_ID[p.kind] or 0,
    value_type = M.VALUE_TYPE_ID[p.value_type or "Unknown"] or 0,
    logical_slot = p.logical_slot or 0,
    value_index = p.value_index or 0,
    pc = p.pc or 0,
    reason = p.reason,
  }
end

function M.lower_to_abi(f)
  local locations, endpoints = {}, {}
  for _, ep in ipairs(f.endpoints or {}) do
    local loc_start = #locations + 1
    for _, loc in ipairs(ep.locations or {}) do locations[#locations + 1] = loc_to_abi(loc) end
    endpoints[#endpoints + 1] = {
      kind = M.ENDPOINT_KIND_ID[ep.kind] or 0,
      flags = 0,
      location_start = loc_start,
      n_locations = #(ep.locations or {}),
      projection_start = ep.projection_start or 0,
      n_projections = ep.n_projections or 0,
    }
  end

  local data_relocs = {}
  for _, r in ipairs(f.data_relocs or {}) do
    data_relocs[#data_relocs + 1] = {
      code_offset = r.code_offset or 0,
      code_offset_kind = r.code_offset_kind,
      reloc_kind = M.RELOC_KIND_ID[r.reloc_kind or "unknown"] or 0,
      role_kind = M.DATA_RELOC_KIND_ID[r.role_kind] or 0,
      op_idx = r.op_idx or 0,
      role_arg = r.role_arg or 0,
      addend = r.addend or 0,
    }
  end

  local control_relocs = {}
  for _, r in ipairs(f.control_relocs or {}) do
    control_relocs[#control_relocs + 1] = {
      code_offset = r.code_offset or 0,
      code_offset_kind = r.code_offset_kind,
      reloc_kind = M.RELOC_KIND_ID[r.reloc_kind or "unknown"] or 0,
      edge_kind = M.CONTROL_RELOC_KIND_ID[r.edge_kind] or 0,
      endpoint_index = r.endpoint_index or 0,
      target_delta = r.target_delta or 0,
    }
  end

  local projections = {}
  for _, p in ipairs(f.projections or {}) do projections[#projections + 1] = proj_to_abi(p) end

  local flags = f.executable and M.STENCIL_FLAG.NATIVE or M.STENCIL_FLAG.ABSTRACT
  local pattern_lit = stable_u64_literal(f.pattern_key)
  local pattern_hi, pattern_lo = u64_parts_from_literal(pattern_lit)
  local selector_lit = sig_literal(f.fact_transfer and f.fact_transfer.selector_sig)
  local required_lit = sig_literal(f.fact_transfer and f.fact_transfer.required_sig)
  local checked_lit = sig_literal(f.fact_transfer and f.fact_transfer.checked_sig)
  local produced_lit = sig_literal(f.fact_transfer and f.fact_transfer.produced_sig)
  local killed_lit = sig_literal(f.fact_transfer and f.fact_transfer.killed_sig)
  local selector_hi, selector_lo = u64_parts_from_literal(selector_lit)
  local required_hi, required_lo = u64_parts_from_literal(required_lit)
  local checked_hi, checked_lo = u64_parts_from_literal(checked_lit)
  local produced_hi, produced_lo = u64_parts_from_literal(produced_lit)
  local killed_hi, killed_lo = u64_parts_from_literal(killed_lit)
  return {
    schema = "spon.stencil.abi.v1",
    executable = f.executable == true,
    layout = f.layout,
    code_hex = f.code_hex,
    code_size = f.code_size or 0,
    stencil = {
      stencil_id = f.stencil_id or 0,
      offset = 0,
      size = f.code_size or 0,
      endpoint_start = 1,
      data_reloc_start = 1,
      control_reloc_start = 1,
      slotmap_start = 1,
      projection_start = 1,
      dependency_start = 1,
      len = f.len or 0,
      n_endpoints = #(f.endpoints or {}),
      n_data_relocs = #(f.data_relocs or {}),
      n_control_relocs = #(f.control_relocs or {}),
      n_slotmaps = #(f.slotmaps or {}),
      n_projections = #(f.projections or {}),
      n_dependencies = #(f.dependencies or {}),
      flags = flags,
      physical_abi = M.PHYSICAL_ABI_ID[f.physical_abi] or 0,
      pattern_key = pattern_lit,
      pattern_key_hi = pattern_hi,
      pattern_key_lo = pattern_lo,
      selector_sig = selector_lit,
      selector_sig_hi = selector_hi,
      selector_sig_lo = selector_lo,
      required_sig = required_lit,
      required_sig_hi = required_hi,
      required_sig_lo = required_lo,
      checked_sig = checked_lit,
      checked_sig_hi = checked_hi,
      checked_sig_lo = checked_lo,
      produced_sig = produced_lit,
      produced_sig_hi = produced_hi,
      produced_sig_lo = produced_lo,
      killed_sig = killed_lit,
      killed_sig_hi = killed_hi,
      killed_sig_lo = killed_lo,
    },
    endpoints = endpoints,
    locations = locations,
    data_relocs = data_relocs,
    control_relocs = control_relocs,
    slotmaps = copy_array(f.slotmaps),
    projections = projections,
    dependencies = copy_array(f.dependencies),
    clobbers = copy_array(f.clobbers),
  }
end

return M

-- stencil_projection.lua -- mandatory exit projection recipes for native stencils.

local M = {}

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

function M.synced_frame(exit, source)
  return {
    kind = "SYNCED_FRAME",
    reason = exit and exit.reason or "exit",
    pc = exit and exit.pc or source or 0,
    entries = {},
  }
end

function M.box_i64(logical_slot, value_index, exit, source)
  return {
    kind = "BOX_I64",
    value_type = "I64",
    logical_slot = logical_slot,
    value_index = value_index,
    reason = exit and exit.reason or "exit",
    pc = exit and exit.pc or source or 0,
    entries = {},
  }
end

function M.for_exit(st, n, config)
  return M.synced_frame(n and n.exit, n and n.source)
end

function M.validate_projection(p)
  if type(p) ~= "table" then return false, "missing projection" end
  if p.kind ~= "SYNCED_FRAME" and p.kind ~= "BOX_I64" then return false, "unknown projection kind: " .. tostring(p.kind) end
  if p.pc == nil then return false, "projection missing pc" end
  if p.kind == "SYNCED_FRAME" and not p.entries then p.entries = {} end
  if p.entries then p.entries = copy_array(p.entries) end
  return true
end

return M

-- stencil_native_x64.lua -- emit executable x86-64 bytes for first SpongeJIT native stencil surface.
--
-- This module is deliberately small and explicit: it emits fixed-width patch
-- sites for canonical slot, immediate, and control relocations. The foundry
-- normal form remains the semantic identity; every per-instance value is a
-- relocation, never baked into representative source operands.

local StencilDesc = require("src.stencil_desc")

local M = {}

local LUA_VNUMINT = 3

local CTX_STACK = 0
local CTX_K = 8
local CTX_EXIT_KIND = 16 + 256 * 16
local CTX_EXIT_PC = CTX_EXIT_KIND + 4
local CTX_EXIT_OP_IDX = CTX_EXIT_KIND + 8
local CTX_EXIT_HOLE = CTX_EXIT_KIND + 12

local SPON_EXIT_NONE = 0
local SPON_EXIT_GUARD = 1
local SPON_EXIT_RESIDUAL = 2
local SPON_EXIT_BOUNDARY = 3
local SPON_EXIT_UNLOWERED = 5

local REG = {
  rax = 0, rcx = 1, rdx = 2, rbx = 3, rsp = 4, rbp = 5, rsi = 6, rdi = 7,
  r8 = 8, r9 = 9, r10 = 10, r11 = 11,
}

local REG_POOL = { "rax", "rcx", "rdx", "r8", "r9", "r10" }

local BINOP = {
  ADD = "add",
  SUB = "sub",
  MUL = "imul",
  BAND = "and",
  BOR = "or",
  BXOR = "xor",
}

local CMP_CC = {
  EQ = 0x94, EQI = 0x94,
  LT = 0x9c, LTI = 0x9c,
  LE = 0x9e, LEI = 0x9e,
  GT = 0x9f, GTI = 0x9f,
  GE = 0x9d, GEI = 0x9d,
}

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function u8s_from_u32(x)
  x = tonumber(x) or 0
  if x < 0 then x = x + 4294967296 end
  return x % 256, math.floor(x / 256) % 256, math.floor(x / 65536) % 256, math.floor(x / 16777216) % 256
end

local function u8s_from_u64(x)
  local n = tonumber(x) or 0
  local sign = n < 0
  if sign then n = -n end
  local bytes = {}
  for i = 1, 8 do
    bytes[i] = n % 256
    n = math.floor(n / 256)
  end
  if sign then
    local carry = 1
    for i = 1, 8 do
      local v = 255 - bytes[i] + carry
      if v >= 256 then v = v - 256; carry = 1 else carry = 0 end
      bytes[i] = v
    end
  end
  return bytes
end

local Asm = {}
Asm.__index = Asm

local function new_asm()
  return setmetatable({ bytes = {}, labels = {}, fixups = {}, errors = {} }, Asm)
end

function Asm:pos() return #self.bytes end
function Asm:emit(...) for i = 1, select("#", ...) do self.bytes[#self.bytes + 1] = select(i, ...) end end
function Asm:mark(name) self.labels[name] = self:pos() end
function Asm:error(msg) self.errors[#self.errors + 1] = msg end

function Asm:patch_u32(off, value)
  local b1, b2, b3, b4 = u8s_from_u32(value)
  self.bytes[off + 1], self.bytes[off + 2], self.bytes[off + 3], self.bytes[off + 4] = b1, b2, b3, b4
end

function Asm:rex(w, r, x, b)
  local v = 0x40
  if w then v = v + 0x08 end
  if r and r >= 8 then v = v + 0x04 end
  if x and x >= 8 then v = v + 0x02 end
  if b and b >= 8 then v = v + 0x01 end
  if v ~= 0x40 then self:emit(v) end
end

function Asm:modrm(mod, reg, rm)
  self:emit(mod * 64 + (reg % 8) * 8 + (rm % 8))
end

function Asm:disp32(v)
  local b1, b2, b3, b4 = u8s_from_u32(v or 0)
  self:emit(b1, b2, b3, b4)
end

function Asm:imm32(v) self:disp32(v) end
function Asm:imm64(v)
  for _, b in ipairs(u8s_from_u64(v or 0)) do self:emit(b) end
end

function Asm:mov_r64_m64(dst, base, disp)
  dst, base = REG[dst], REG[base]
  self:rex(true, dst, nil, base); self:emit(0x8b); self:modrm(2, dst, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_m64_r64(base, disp, src)
  base, src = REG[base], REG[src]
  self:rex(true, src, nil, base); self:emit(0x89); self:modrm(2, src, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_r64_r64(dst, src)
  dst, src = REG[dst], REG[src]
  if dst == src then return end
  self:rex(true, src, nil, dst); self:emit(0x89); self:modrm(3, src, dst)
end

function Asm:movabs_r64(dst, imm)
  dst = REG[dst]
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, dst); self:emit(0xb8 + (dst % 8)); local off = self:pos(); self:imm64(imm or 0); return off
end

function Asm:cmp_m8_imm8(base, disp, imm)
  base = REG[base]
  self:rex(false, nil, nil, base); self:emit(0x80); self:modrm(2, 7, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); local imm_off = self:pos(); self:emit(imm or 0); return off, imm_off
end

function Asm:mov_r8_m8(dst, base, disp)
  dst, base = REG[dst], REG[base]
  self:rex(false, dst, nil, base); self:emit(0x8a); self:modrm(2, dst, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_m8_r8(base, disp, src)
  base, src = REG[base], REG[src]
  self:rex(false, src, nil, base); self:emit(0x88); self:modrm(2, src, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_m8_imm8(base, disp, imm)
  base = REG[base]
  self:rex(false, nil, nil, base); self:emit(0xc6); self:modrm(2, 0, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); local imm_off = self:pos(); self:emit(imm or 0); return off, imm_off
end

function Asm:mov_m64_imm32(base, disp, imm)
  base = REG[base]
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, base); self:emit(0xc7); self:modrm(2, 0, base); local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(disp or 0); self:imm32(imm or 0); return off
end

function Asm:mov_m32_imm32(base, disp, imm)
  base = REG[base]
  self:rex(false, nil, nil, base); self:emit(0xc7); self:modrm(2, 0, base); self:disp32(disp or 0); self:imm32(imm or 0)
end

function Asm:bin_r64_r64(op, dst, src)
  local opcode = ({ add = 0x01, sub = 0x29, ["and"] = 0x21, ["or"] = 0x09, ["xor"] = 0x31 })[op]
  dst, src = REG[dst], REG[src]
  if op == "imul" then
    self:rex(true, dst, nil, src); self:emit(0x0f, 0xaf); self:modrm(3, dst, src)
  elseif opcode then
    self:rex(true, src, nil, dst); self:emit(opcode); self:modrm(3, src, dst)
  else
    self:error("unsupported x64 binop: " .. tostring(op))
  end
end

function Asm:unary_r64(op, reg)
  local ext = ({ UNM = 3, BNOT = 2 })[op]
  reg = REG[reg]
  if not ext then self:error("unsupported x64 unary op: " .. tostring(op)); return end
  self:rex(true, nil, nil, reg); self:emit(0xf7); self:modrm(3, ext, reg)
end

function Asm:cmp_r64_r64(left, right)
  left, right = REG[left], REG[right]
  self:rex(true, right, nil, left); self:emit(0x39); self:modrm(3, right, left)
end

function Asm:test_r64_r64(reg)
  reg = REG[reg]
  self:rex(true, reg, nil, reg); self:emit(0x85); self:modrm(3, reg, reg)
end

function Asm:setcc_r8(cc, reg)
  reg = REG[reg]
  self:rex(false, nil, nil, reg); self:emit(0x0f, cc); self:modrm(3, 0, reg)
end

function Asm:movzx_r64_r8(dst, src)
  dst, src = REG[dst], REG[src]
  self:rex(true, dst, nil, src); self:emit(0x0f, 0xb6); self:modrm(3, dst, src)
end

function Asm:jcc32(cc, label)
  self:emit(0x0f, cc)
  local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(0)
  self.fixups[#self.fixups + 1] = { off = off, label = label }
  return off
end

function Asm:jmp32(label)
  self:emit(0xe9)
  local off = self:pos(); self:disp32(0)
  self.fixups[#self.fixups + 1] = { off = off, label = label }
  return off
end

function Asm:ret() self:emit(0xc3) end

function Asm:finish()
  for _, f in ipairs(self.fixups) do
    local target = self.labels[f.label]
    if not target then self:error("missing label: " .. tostring(f.label))
    else self:patch_u32(f.off, target - (f.off + 4)) end
  end
  if #self.errors > 0 then return nil, self.errors end
  local chars = {}
  for i, b in ipairs(self.bytes) do chars[i] = string.char(b) end
  return table.concat(chars)
end

local function bytes_to_hex(bytes)
  local out = {}
  for i = 1, #bytes do out[#out + 1] = string.format("%02x", bytes:byte(i)) end
  return table.concat(out)
end

local function use_counts(st)
  local counts = {}
  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do counts[v] = (counts[v] or 0) + 1 end
  end
  return counts
end

local function slot_default_disp(h, addend)
  return (tonumber(h and h.role_arg) or 0) * 16 + (addend or 0)
end

local function make_data_reloc(out, code_offset, reloc_kind, h, addend)
  out[#out + 1] = StencilDesc.data_reloc({
    code_offset = code_offset,
    code_offset_kind = "x86_64_patch_site",
    reloc_kind = reloc_kind,
    role_kind = h and h.role_kind,
    op_idx = h and h.op_idx or 0,
    role_arg = h and h.role_arg or 0,
    hole_id = h and h.id,
    value_type = h and h.ty,
    addend = addend or 0,
  })
end

local function op_exit_kind(n)
  if n.op == "GuardI64" then return SPON_EXIT_GUARD end
  if n.op == "ExitResidual" then return SPON_EXIT_RESIDUAL end
  if n.op == "ExitBoundary" then return SPON_EXIT_BOUNDARY end
  if n.op == "ExitUnlowered" then return SPON_EXIT_UNLOWERED end
  return SPON_EXIT_BOUNDARY
end

local function control_edge_kind(n)
  if n.op == "GuardI64" then return "guard_fail" end
  if n.op == "ExitResidual" then return "residual" end
  return "boundary"
end

function M.emit(st, opts)
  opts = opts or {}
  local errors = {}
  local a = new_asm()
  local data_relocs, control_relocs = {}, {}
  local counts = use_counts(st)
  local loc, free = {}, {}
  for _, r in ipairs(REG_POOL) do free[r] = true end

  local function alloc(v)
    for _, r in ipairs(REG_POOL) do
      if free[r] then free[r] = nil; if v then loc[v] = { kind = "reg", reg = r } end; return r end
    end
    errors[#errors + 1] = "native x64 register budget exceeded"
    return nil
  end

  local function free_reg(r)
    if r and r ~= "r11" and REG[r] and not free[r] then free[r] = true end
  end

  local function consume(v)
    if not v then return end
    counts[v] = (counts[v] or 0) - 1
    if counts[v] <= 0 then
      local l = loc[v]
      if l and l.kind == "reg" then free_reg(l.reg) end
    end
  end

  local function input_reg(v)
    local l = loc[v]
    if not l then errors[#errors + 1] = "missing native value location: " .. tostring(v); return nil end
    if l.kind ~= "reg" then errors[#errors + 1] = "native value is not in register: " .. tostring(v); return nil end
    return l.reg
  end

  local function value_ty(v)
    return st.values and st.values[v] and st.values[v].ty or "Unknown"
  end

  local function input_slot(v)
    local l = loc[v]
    if not l then errors[#errors + 1] = "missing native slot value: " .. tostring(v); return nil end
    if l.kind ~= "slot" then errors[#errors + 1] = "native value is not a frame slot: " .. tostring(v); return nil end
    return l.hole
  end

  local function ensure_stub(n)
    local ep_idx = opts.exit_endpoint_by_node and opts.exit_endpoint_by_node[n.id]
    if not ep_idx then errors[#errors + 1] = "missing endpoint for native control op " .. tostring(n.id); return nil end
    return "exit_ep_" .. tostring(ep_idx), ep_idx
  end

  local stubs = {}
  local function record_stub(label, n)
    if not stubs[label] then stubs[label] = n end
  end

  local bool_label_counter = 0
  local function store_tvalue(dst_hole, src_loc, src_value)
    if not dst_hole or not src_loc then return end
    local function dst_reloc(off, addend) make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs32s", dst_hole, addend) end

    if src_loc.kind == "slot" then
      local sh = src_loc.hole
      local off_src_v = a:mov_r64_m64("rax", "r11", slot_default_disp(sh, 0))
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_src_v, "abs32s", sh, 0)
      local off_dst_v = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), "rax")
      dst_reloc(off_dst_v, 0)
      local off_src_t = a:mov_r8_m8("rax", "r11", slot_default_disp(sh, 8))
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_src_t, "abs32s", sh, 8)
      local off_dst_t = a:mov_m8_r8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), "rax")
      dst_reloc(off_dst_t, 8)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "const_nil" then
      local off_v = a:mov_m64_imm32("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), 0)
      dst_reloc(off_v, 0)
      local off_t = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), 0)
      dst_reloc(off_t, 8)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "const_bool" then
      local off_v = a:mov_m64_imm32("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), 0)
      dst_reloc(off_v, 0)
      local default_tag = (src_loc.default == false) and 1 or 17
      local off_t, imm_off = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), default_tag)
      dst_reloc(off_t, 8)
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, imm_off, "abs8", src_loc.hole, 0)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "const_pool" then
      local kh = src_loc.hole
      a:mov_r64_m64("r10", "rdi", CTX_K)
      local off_kv = a:mov_r64_m64("rax", "r10", slot_default_disp(kh, 0))
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_kv, "abs32s", kh, 0)
      local off_dv = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), "rax")
      dst_reloc(off_dv, 0)
      local off_kt = a:mov_r8_m8("rax", "r10", slot_default_disp(kh, 8))
      make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_kt, "abs32s", kh, 8)
      local off_dt = a:mov_m8_r8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), "rax")
      dst_reloc(off_dt, 8)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "reg" and value_ty(src_value) == "I64" then
      local off_v = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), src_loc.reg)
      dst_reloc(off_v, 0)
      local off_t = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), LUA_VNUMINT)
      dst_reloc(off_t, 8)

    elseif src_loc.kind == "reg" and value_ty(src_value) == "Bool" then
      local off_v = a:mov_m64_imm32("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 0), 0)
      dst_reloc(off_v, 0)
      a:test_r64_r64(src_loc.reg)
      local off_false = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), 1)
      dst_reloc(off_false, 8)
      bool_label_counter = bool_label_counter + 1
      local done = "bool_store_done_" .. tostring(bool_label_counter)
      a:jcc32(0x84, done) -- JE: zero/false keeps false tag.
      local off_true = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(dst_hole, 8), 17)
      dst_reloc(off_true, 8)
      a:mark(done)

    else
      errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native StoreSlot input: " .. tostring(src_loc.kind) .. ":" .. tostring(value_ty(src_value))
    end
  end

  -- Keep ctx in rdi for the full native stencil. r11 caches ctx->stack.
  a:mov_r64_m64("r11", "rdi", CTX_STACK)

  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    local op = n.op
    if op == "LoadSlot" then
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = { kind = "slot", hole = n.hole } end

    elseif op == "LoadConst" then
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = { kind = "const_pool", hole = n.hole } end

    elseif op == "ConstNil" then
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = { kind = "const_nil" } end

    elseif op == "ConstBool" then
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = { kind = "const_bool", hole = n.hole, default = n.args and n.args.value } end

    elseif op == "Move" then
      local src = loc[n.inputs and n.inputs[1]]
      if not src then errors[#errors + 1] = "missing native move input: " .. tostring(n.inputs and n.inputs[1]) end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = src end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "GuardI64" then
      local h = input_slot(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      if h then
        local off = a:cmp_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(h, 8), LUA_VNUMINT)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs32s", h, 8)
        local label, ep_idx = ensure_stub(n)
        if label then
          local coff = a:jcc32(0x85, label) -- JNE
          control_relocs[#control_relocs + 1] = StencilDesc.control_reloc({
            code_offset = coff,
            code_offset_kind = "x86_64_rel32",
            reloc_kind = "pc32",
            edge_kind = "guard_fail",
            endpoint_index = ep_idx,
            source = n.source,
          })
          record_stub(label, n)
        end
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "UnboxI64" then
      local h = input_slot(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local r = alloc(n.outputs and n.outputs[1])
      if h and r then
        local off = a:mov_r64_m64(r, "r11", slot_default_disp(h, 0))
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs32s", h, 0)
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "ConstI64" then
      local r = alloc(n.outputs and n.outputs[1])
      if r then a:movabs_r64(r, tonumber(n.args and n.args.value) or 0) end

    elseif op == "ConstI64Hole" then
      local r = alloc(n.outputs and n.outputs[1])
      if r then
        local off = a:movabs_r64(r, 0)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off, "abs64", n.hole, 0)
      end

    elseif op == "AddI64" or op == "SubI64" or op == "MulI64" or op == "I64BinOp" then
      local lhs = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local rhs = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])
      local bop = (op == "AddI64" and "ADD") or (op == "SubI64" and "SUB") or (op == "MulI64" and "MUL") or (n.args and n.args.op)
      local xop = BINOP[bop]
      if not xop then errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native x64 i64 binop: " .. tostring(bop)
      elseif lhs and rhs then
        local out = n.outputs and n.outputs[1]
        loc[out] = { kind = "reg", reg = lhs }
        a:bin_r64_r64(xop, lhs, rhs)
      end
      consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[1]); consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])

    elseif op == "I64UnaryOp" then
      local src = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local uop = n.args and n.args.op
      if src then
        loc[n.outputs and n.outputs[1]] = { kind = "reg", reg = src }
        a:unary_r64(uop, src)
      end
      consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])

    elseif op == "CmpI64" then
      local lhs = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local rhs = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])
      local cc = CMP_CC[n.args and n.args.cmp_op or "EQ"]
      if not cc then errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native x64 i64 cmp: " .. tostring(n.args and n.args.cmp_op)
      elseif lhs and rhs then
        loc[n.outputs and n.outputs[1]] = { kind = "reg", reg = lhs }
        a:cmp_r64_r64(lhs, rhs)
        a:setcc_r8(cc, lhs)
        a:movzx_r64_r8(lhs, lhs)
      end
      consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[1]); consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])

    elseif op == "StoreSlot" then
      local input = n.inputs and n.inputs[1]
      store_tvalue(n.hole, loc[input], input)
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "StoreI64Slot" then
      local src = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local h = n.hole
      if src and h then
        local off_v = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(h, 0), src)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_v, "abs32s", h, 0)
        local off_t = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(h, 8), LUA_VNUMINT)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_t, "abs32s", h, 8)
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "ExitResidual" or op == "ExitBoundary" or op == "ExitUnlowered" then
      local label, ep_idx = ensure_stub(n)
      if label then
        local coff = a:jmp32(label)
        control_relocs[#control_relocs + 1] = StencilDesc.control_reloc({
          code_offset = coff,
          code_offset_kind = "x86_64_rel32",
          reloc_kind = "pc32",
          edge_kind = control_edge_kind(n),
          endpoint_index = ep_idx,
          source = n.source,
        })
        record_stub(label, n)
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    else
      errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native x64 stencil op: " .. tostring(op)
    end
  end

  -- Success is an explicit patchable fallthrough edge. If a materializer does
  -- not patch it, the rel32 targets the local ok endpoint stub below.
  local ok_endpoint_index = opts.ok_endpoint_index or 2
  local ok_label = "ok_ep_" .. tostring(ok_endpoint_index)
  local ok_off = a:jmp32(ok_label)
  control_relocs[#control_relocs + 1] = StencilDesc.control_reloc({
    code_offset = ok_off,
    code_offset_kind = "x86_64_rel32",
    reloc_kind = "pc32",
    edge_kind = "fallthrough",
    endpoint_index = ok_endpoint_index,
    source = 0,
  })

  a:mark(ok_label)
  a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_KIND, SPON_EXIT_NONE)
  a:ret()

  local labels = {}
  for label in pairs(stubs) do labels[#labels + 1] = label end
  table.sort(labels)
  for _, label in ipairs(labels) do
    local n = stubs[label]
    local source = tonumber(n.source or 0) or 0
    a:mark(label)
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_KIND, op_exit_kind(n))
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_PC, source)
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_OP_IDX, math.max(0, source - 1))
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_HOLE, 0)
    a:ret()
  end

  for _, e in ipairs(errors) do a:error(e) end
  local bytes, ferrs = a:finish()
  if not bytes then return nil, ferrs end

  return {
    bytes = bytes,
    code_hex = bytes_to_hex(bytes),
    code_size = #bytes,
    data_relocs = data_relocs,
    control_relocs = control_relocs,
    layout = {
      mode = "native_bytes",
      executable = true,
      arch = "x86_64",
      encoding = "raw_function_body",
      entry_offset = 0,
      code_size = #bytes,
      fixed_width_patch_sites = true,
    },
  }
end

M.CTX_OFFSETS = {
  stack = CTX_STACK,
  exit_kind = CTX_EXIT_KIND,
  exit_pc = CTX_EXIT_PC,
  exit_op_idx = CTX_EXIT_OP_IDX,
  exit_hole = CTX_EXIT_HOLE,
}

return M

-- materialize_native_x64.lua -- mechanical copy/link/patch for SpongeJIT x86-64 native stencils.
--
-- This module consumes already-built native stencil descriptors. It never runs
-- SSA, never normalizes, and never infers hidden semantics. Per-site variation
-- enters only through explicit relocation records plus caller-provided concrete
-- bytecode/slot/constant bindings.

local M = {}

local RELOC_KIND_BY_ID = {
  [0] = "unknown", [1] = "abs32", [2] = "abs32s", [3] = "plt32", [4] = "pc32", [5] = "abs64", [6] = "abs8",
}

local DATA_ROLE_BY_ID = {
  [1] = "slot", [2] = "slot_store", [3] = "imm", [4] = "const", [5] = "bool",
  [6] = "shape_offset", [7] = "shape_id", [8] = "metatable_offset", [9] = "field_offset",
  [10] = "array_base_offset", [11] = "call_target", [12] = "barrier",
}

local CONTROL_EDGE_BY_ID = {
  [1] = "fallthrough", [2] = "guard_fail", [3] = "residual", [4] = "boundary", [5] = "projection_stub",
}

local function kind_name(v, by_id)
  if type(v) == "string" then return v end
  return by_id[tonumber(v) or -1] or tostring(v)
end

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function hex_to_array(hex)
  assert(type(hex) == "string" and #hex % 2 == 0, "native stencil code_hex must be an even-length hex string")
  local out = {}
  for pair in hex:gmatch("%x%x") do out[#out + 1] = tonumber(pair, 16) or 0 end
  return out
end

local function array_to_string(bytes)
  local out = {}
  for i, b in ipairs(bytes or {}) do out[i] = string.char(b % 256) end
  return table.concat(out)
end

local function string_to_array(s)
  local out = {}
  for i = 1, #s do out[i] = s:byte(i) end
  return out
end

local function patch_u8(bytes, off, v)
  bytes[off + 1] = (tonumber(v) or 0) % 256
end

local function patch_u32(bytes, off, v)
  local n = tonumber(v) or 0
  if n < 0 then n = n + 4294967296 end
  bytes[off + 1] = n % 256
  bytes[off + 2] = math.floor(n / 256) % 256
  bytes[off + 3] = math.floor(n / 65536) % 256
  bytes[off + 4] = math.floor(n / 16777216) % 256
end

local function patch_i64(bytes, off, v)
  local n = tonumber(v) or 0
  local neg = n < 0
  if neg then n = -n end
  local b = {}
  for i = 1, 8 do b[i] = n % 256; n = math.floor(n / 256) end
  if neg then
    local carry = 1
    for i = 1, 8 do
      local x = 255 - b[i] + carry
      if x >= 256 then x = x - 256; carry = 1 else carry = 0 end
      b[i] = x
    end
  end
  for i = 1, 8 do bytes[off + i] = b[i] end
end

local function op_at(entry, op_idx)
  return (entry.ops or entry.source_ops or {})[(tonumber(op_idx) or 0) + 1] or {}
end

local function op_name(op)
  if type(op) == "table" then return tostring(op.op or "") end
  return tostring(op or "")
end

local function logical_slot(entry, role_arg)
  local logical = tonumber(role_arg) or 0
  local slots = entry.actual_slots or entry.slot_bindings or entry.slots or {}
  if slots[logical] ~= nil then return tonumber(slots[logical]) or logical end
  if slots[tostring(logical)] ~= nil then return tonumber(slots[tostring(logical)]) or logical end
  return logical
end

local function default_imm_from_op(op)
  if type(op) ~= "table" then return 0 end
  if op.c ~= nil then return tonumber(op.c) or 0 end
  if op.sC ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sC) or 0 end
  if op.b ~= nil then return tonumber(op.b) or 0 end
  if op.sB ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sB) or 0 end
  if op.sBx ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sBx) or 0 end
  if op.sJ ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sJ) or 0 end
  return 0
end

local function imm_value(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local imms = entry.immediates or {}
  if imms[op_idx] ~= nil then return imms[op_idx] end
  if imms[tostring(op_idx)] ~= nil then return imms[tostring(op_idx)] end
  return default_imm_from_op(op_at(entry, op_idx))
end

local function const_index(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local consts = entry.const_slots or entry.constants or {}
  if consts[op_idx] ~= nil then return tonumber(consts[op_idx]) or 0 end
  if consts[tostring(op_idx)] ~= nil then return tonumber(consts[tostring(op_idx)]) or 0 end
  local op = op_at(entry, op_idx)
  if type(op) == "table" then return tonumber(op.bx or op.ax or op.b or op.c or op.const or 0) or 0 end
  return 0
end

local function bool_tag(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local bools = entry.booleans or {}
  local v = bools[op_idx]
  if v == nil then v = bools[tostring(op_idx)] end
  if v == nil then
    local name = op_name(op_at(entry, op_idx))
    if name == "LOADFALSE" or name == "LFALSESKIP" then v = false else v = true end
  end
  return v and 17 or 1 -- PUC Lua 5.5 LUA_VTRUE / LUA_VFALSE tags.
end

local function data_patch_value(entry, r)
  local role = kind_name(r.role_kind, DATA_ROLE_BY_ID)
  local addend = tonumber(r.addend) or 0
  if role == "slot" or role == "slot_store" then
    return logical_slot(entry, r.role_arg) * 16 + addend
  elseif role == "imm" then
    return imm_value(entry, r) + addend
  elseif role == "const" then
    return const_index(entry, r) * 16 + addend
  elseif role == "bool" then
    return bool_tag(entry, r) + addend
  end
  error("unsupported materializer data role: " .. tostring(role))
end

local function patch_data_reloc(bytes, base, entry, r)
  local reloc = kind_name(r.reloc_kind, RELOC_KIND_BY_ID)
  local off = base + (tonumber(r.code_offset) or 0)
  local v = data_patch_value(entry, r)
  if reloc == "abs8" then patch_u8(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "abs32" or reloc == "abs32s" then patch_u32(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "abs64" then patch_i64(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "unknown" then error("native data reloc has unknown encoding at offset " .. tostring(off))
  else error("unsupported materializer data reloc encoding: " .. tostring(reloc)) end
end

local function normalize_entry(e)
  if e.stencil then return e end
  return { stencil = e }
end

function M.materialize(entries, opts)
  opts = opts or {}
  assert(type(entries) == "table" and #entries > 0, "materialize requires at least one stencil entry")
  local norm = {}
  local image = {}
  local offset = 0

  for i, raw in ipairs(entries) do
    local entry = normalize_entry(raw)
    local st = assert(entry.stencil, "entry missing stencil")
    if st.executable ~= true then error("cannot materialize non-executable stencil at entry " .. tostring(i)) end
    if type(st.code_hex) ~= "string" or #st.code_hex == 0 then error("native stencil missing code_hex at entry " .. tostring(i)) end
    local bytes = hex_to_array(st.code_hex)
    norm[i] = {
      stencil = st,
      ops = entry.ops or st.source_ops or {},
      source_ops = entry.source_ops or st.source_ops or {},
      actual_slots = entry.actual_slots or entry.slot_bindings or entry.slots,
      slot_bindings = entry.slot_bindings,
      immediates = entry.immediates,
      const_slots = entry.const_slots,
      constants = entry.constants,
      booleans = entry.booleans,
      base = offset,
      size = #bytes,
    }
    for _, b in ipairs(bytes) do image[#image + 1] = b end
    offset = offset + #bytes
  end

  for _, entry in ipairs(norm) do
    for _, r in ipairs(entry.stencil.data_relocs or {}) do patch_data_reloc(image, entry.base, entry, r) end
  end

  for i, entry in ipairs(norm) do
    for _, r in ipairs(entry.stencil.control_relocs or {}) do
      local edge = kind_name(r.edge_kind, CONTROL_EDGE_BY_ID)
      local reloc = kind_name(r.reloc_kind, RELOC_KIND_BY_ID)
      if edge == "fallthrough" and i < #norm then
        if reloc ~= "pc32" then error("fallthrough control reloc must be pc32") end
        local site = entry.base + (tonumber(r.code_offset) or 0)
        local target = norm[i + 1].base
        patch_u32(image, site, target - (site + 4))
      end
    end
  end

  local bytes = array_to_string(image)
  return {
    ok = true,
    bytes = bytes,
    code_size = #bytes,
    entries = norm,
  }
end

function M.alloc_executable(bytes)
  local ok, ffi = pcall(require, "ffi")
  if not ok then return nil, "ffi unavailable" end
  ffi.cdef[[
    typedef unsigned long size_t;
    void *mmap(void *addr, size_t length, int prot, int flags, int fd, long offset);
    int munmap(void *addr, size_t length);
    void *memcpy(void *dest, const void *src, size_t n);
  ]]
  local PROT_READ, PROT_WRITE, PROT_EXEC = 1, 2, 4
  local MAP_PRIVATE, MAP_ANON = 2, 0x20
  local n = #bytes
  local mem = ffi.C.mmap(nil, n, PROT_READ + PROT_WRITE + PROT_EXEC, MAP_PRIVATE + MAP_ANON, -1, 0)
  if mem == ffi.cast("void *", -1) then return nil, "mmap failed" end
  local src = ffi.new("uint8_t[?]", n)
  local arr = string_to_array(bytes)
  for i, b in ipairs(arr) do src[i - 1] = b end
  ffi.C.memcpy(mem, src, n)
  return mem, n
end

function M.free_executable(mem, n)
  local ok, ffi = pcall(require, "ffi")
  if not ok then return nil, "ffi unavailable" end
  return ffi.C.munmap(mem, n) == 0
end

M._patch_u32 = patch_u32
M._patch_i64 = patch_i64
M._hex_to_array = hex_to_array
M._array_to_string = array_to_string

-- build_bank.lua -- Generate a C metadata bank from the global SQLite form DB.
--
-- This consumes build/stencil_bank/stencil_bank.sqlite from build_stencils.sh
-- and emits libsponbank.so exposing the sponbank.h descriptor/selector API.
-- Native stencil rows carry raw byte bodies plus explicit data/control relocs.

package.path = "../../../?.lua;../../../?/init.lua;src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local cjson = require("cjson")
local sqlite3 = require("lsqlite3")
local Util = require("src.util")
local Constants = require("experiments.lua_interpreter_vm.src.constants")

local M = {}

local U32 = 4294967296

local function q(s) return Util.shell_quote(s) end

local function mkdir_p(path) assert(Util.mkdir_p(path), "mkdir failed: " .. tostring(path)) end

local function c_string(s)
  s = tostring(s or "")
  s = s:gsub("\\", "\\\\"):gsub('"', '\\"'):gsub("\n", "\\n"):gsub("\r", "\\r"):gsub("\t", "\\t")
  return '"' .. s .. '"'
end

local function as_c_u64_literal(x)
  if type(x) == "string" and x:match("^0x[%x]+ULL$") then return x end
  if type(x) == "string" and x:match("^0x[%x]+$") then return x .. "ULL" end
  return "0x0000000000000000ULL"
end

local function parse_sig_lohi(lit)
  local hex = tostring(lit or "0"):match("^0x([%x]+)ULL$") or tostring(lit or "0"):match("^0x([%x]+)$") or "0"
  hex = hex:lower()
  if #hex > 16 then hex = hex:sub(#hex - 15) end
  hex = string.rep("0", 16 - #hex) .. hex
  return tonumber(hex:sub(9, 16), 16) or 0, tonumber(hex:sub(1, 8), 16) or 0
end

local function popcount32(x)
  x = tonumber(x) or 0
  local n = 0
  while x > 0 do
    if x % 2 >= 1 then n = n + 1 end
    x = math.floor(x / 2)
  end
  return n
end

local function sig_pop(lit)
  local lo, hi = parse_sig_lohi(lit)
  return popcount32(lo) + popcount32(hi)
end

local OPCODE = {}
for name, value in pairs(Constants.Op or {}) do
  if type(name) == "string" and type(value) == "number" then OPCODE[name] = value end
end

local function op_name(op)
  if type(op) == "table" then return tostring(op.op or "") end
  return tostring(op or "")
end

local function packed_pattern(ops)
  local packed = 0
  for i, op in ipairs(ops or {}) do
    if i > 4 then break end
    local code = OPCODE[op_name(op)] or 0
    packed = packed + code * (256 ^ (i - 1))
  end
  return packed
end

local function selector_key_num(ops)
  local len = #(ops or {})
  return len * U32 + packed_pattern(ops)
end

local function selector_key_lit(ops)
  local len = #(ops or {})
  return string.format("0x%x%08xULL", len, packed_pattern(ops))
end

local function chunk_path(bank_src, name)
  if name:sub(1, 1) == "/" then return name end
  return bank_src .. "/" .. name
end

local function open_inc(out_dir, prefix, suffix)
  local path = string.format("%s/%s_%s.inc", out_dir, prefix, suffix)
  local f = assert(io.open(path, "wb"))
  return f, path
end

local function write_array_header(c, ctype, name, dummy)
  c:write("const ", ctype, " ", name, "[] = {\n")
  if dummy then c:write("  ", dummy, "\n") end
end

local function write_include(c, path)
  c:write('#include "', Util.basename(path), '"\n')
end

local function write_array_footer(c)
  c:write("};\n\n")
end

local function hex_to_bytes(hex)
  if type(hex) ~= "string" then return {} end
  local out = {}
  for pair in hex:gmatch("%x%x") do out[#out + 1] = tonumber(pair, 16) or 0 end
  return out
end

local function u(v) return tonumber(v) or 0 end
local function field(obj, name, default) local v = obj and obj[name]; if v == nil then return default end; return v end

local function emit_index(out_dir, shards, patterns, l0, total_stencils)
  local pattern_keys = {}
  for k, _ in pairs(patterns) do pattern_keys[#pattern_keys + 1] = k end
  table.sort(pattern_keys)

  local c_path = out_dir .. "/bank_index.c"
  local c = assert(io.open(c_path, "wb"))
  c:write("/* Generated by src/build_bank.lua. Native stencil bank index. */\n")
  c:write('#include "sponbank.h"\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n\n')

  for _, s in ipairs(shards) do
    local i = s.chunk
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponStencilDesc spon_chunk_%d_stencils[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const unsigned char spon_chunk_%d_data[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponEndpointDesc spon_chunk_%d_endpoints[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponLocationDesc spon_chunk_%d_locations[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponDataReloc spon_chunk_%d_data_relocs[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponControlReloc spon_chunk_%d_control_relocs[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponSlotMapEntry spon_chunk_%d_slotmaps[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponProjectionEntry spon_chunk_%d_projections[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponDependencyDesc spon_chunk_%d_dependencies[];\n", i))
  end

  c:write([[

typedef struct {
  uint32_t first;
  uint32_t count;
  const SponStencilDesc *stencils;
  const unsigned char *data;
  const SponEndpointDesc *endpoints;
  const SponLocationDesc *locations;
  const SponDataReloc *data_relocs;
  const SponControlReloc *control_relocs;
  const SponSlotMapEntry *slotmaps;
  const SponProjectionEntry *projections;
  const SponDependencyDesc *dependencies;
} SponBankShard;

]])

  c:write("static const SponBankShard spon_shards[] = {\n")
  for _, s in ipairs(shards) do
    local i = s.chunk
    c:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, spon_chunk_%d_stencils, spon_chunk_%d_data, spon_chunk_%d_endpoints, spon_chunk_%d_locations, spon_chunk_%d_data_relocs, spon_chunk_%d_control_relocs, spon_chunk_%d_slotmaps, spon_chunk_%d_projections, spon_chunk_%d_dependencies},\n",
      s.first, s.count, i, i, i, i, i, i, i, i, i))
  end
  c:write("};\n")
  c:write(string.format("static const uint32_t spon_shard_count = %d;\n", #shards))
  c:write(string.format("static const uint32_t spon_stencil_count = %d;\n\n", total_stencils))

  c:write("static const SponStencilId spon_l0[256] = {\n")
  for i = 0, 255 do
    if i % 8 == 0 then c:write("  ") end
    c:write(tostring(l0[i] or 0))
    if i ~= 255 then c:write(", ") end
    if i % 8 == 7 then c:write("\n") end
  end
  c:write("};\n\n")

  c:write("typedef struct { uint64_t pattern_key; uint32_t start; uint32_t count; } PatternEntry;\n")
  c:write("static const SponStencilId spon_candidates[] = {\n")
  local candidate_count = 0
  for _, k in ipairs(pattern_keys) do
    local list = patterns[k]
    table.sort(list, function(a, b) return a > b end)
    for _, packed in ipairs(list) do
      local id = math.floor(packed % U32)
      if candidate_count % 12 == 0 then c:write("  ") end
      c:write(tostring(id), ", ")
      candidate_count = candidate_count + 1
      if candidate_count % 12 == 0 then c:write("\n") end
    end
  end
  if candidate_count % 12 ~= 0 then c:write("\n") end
  c:write("};\n")

  c:write("static const PatternEntry spon_patterns[] = {\n")
  local start = 0
  for _, k in ipairs(pattern_keys) do
    local list = patterns[k]
    local len = math.floor(k / U32)
    local packed = math.floor(k - len * U32)
    c:write(string.format("  {0x%x%08xULL, %d, %d},\n", len, packed, start, #list))
    start = start + #list
  end
  c:write("};\n")
  c:write(string.format("static const uint32_t spon_pattern_count = %d;\n\n", #pattern_keys))

  c:write([[
static const SponBankShard *find_shard(SponStencilId id) {
  uint32_t lo = 0, hi = spon_shard_count;
  while (lo < hi) {
    uint32_t mid = lo + ((hi - lo) >> 1);
    const SponBankShard *s = &spon_shards[mid];
    if (id < s->first) hi = mid;
    else if (id >= s->first + s->count) lo = mid + 1;
    else return s;
  }
  return NULL;
}

static const PatternEntry *find_pattern(uint64_t key) {
  uint32_t lo = 0, hi = spon_pattern_count;
  while (lo < hi) {
    uint32_t mid = lo + ((hi - lo) >> 1);
    uint64_t mk = spon_patterns[mid].pattern_key;
    if (mk == key) return &spon_patterns[mid];
    if (mk < key) lo = mid + 1; else hi = mid;
  }
  return NULL;
}

static uint64_t make_pattern_key(const uint32_t *bc, uint32_t pc, uint32_t len) {
  uint32_t packed = 0;
  for (uint32_t i = 0; i < len; i++) packed |= ((bc[pc + i] & 0xffu) << (i * 8));
  return (((uint64_t)len) << 32) | (uint64_t)packed;
}

uint32_t spon_bank_stencil_count(void) { return spon_stencil_count; }
