local abi = assert(stencil.abi, "stencil row missing abi")
    local af = assert(abi.stencil, "stencil row missing abi.stencil")
    local len = u(af.len ~= nil and af.len or stencil.len)
    local pattern_lit = as_c_u64_literal(af.pattern_key)
    local selector_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.selector_sig)
    local required_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.required_sig)
    local checked_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.checked_sig)
    local produced_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.produced_sig)
    local killed_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.killed_sig)

    local code_bytes = hex_to_bytes(stencil.code_hex or abi.code_hex)
    local byte_start = (#code_bytes > 0) and counts.bytes or 0
    local byte_size = #code_bytes
    local endpoints = abi.endpoints or {}
    local locations = abi.locations or {}
    local data_relocs = abi.data_relocs or {}
    local control_relocs = abi.control_relocs or {}
    local slotmaps = abi.slotmaps or {}
    local projections = abi.projections or {}
    local dependencies = abi.dependencies or {}

    local endpoint_start = (#endpoints > 0) and (counts.endpoints + 1) or 0
    local location_start = (#locations > 0) and (counts.locations + 1) or 0
    local data_reloc_start = (#data_relocs > 0) and (counts.data_relocs + 1) or 0
    local control_reloc_start = (#control_relocs > 0) and (counts.control_relocs + 1) or 0
    local slotmap_start = (#slotmaps > 0) and (counts.slotmaps + 1) or 0
    local projection_start = (#projections > 0) and (counts.projections + 1) or 0
    local dependency_start = (#dependencies > 0) and (counts.dependencies + 1) or 0

    inc.stencils:write(string.format(
      "  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, 0, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s}, /* %s */\n",
      stencil_id, byte_start, byte_size, endpoint_start, data_reloc_start, control_reloc_start, slotmap_start, projection_start,
      dependency_start, len, #endpoints, #data_relocs, #control_relocs, #slotmaps, #projections, #dependencies,
      u(af.flags or 1), u(af.physical_abi or 1), pattern_lit, selector_sig, required_sig, checked_sig, produced_sig,
      killed_sig, tostring(stencil.name or "stencil")))

    for i, b in ipairs(code_bytes) do
      if counts.bytes % 16 == 0 then inc.data_bytes:write("  ") end
      inc.data_bytes:write(string.format("0x%02x, ", b))
      counts.bytes = counts.bytes + 1
      if counts.bytes % 16 == 0 then inc.data_bytes:write("\n") end
    end
    if #code_bytes > 0 and counts.bytes % 16 ~= 0 then inc.data_bytes:write("\n") end

    for _, loc in ipairs(locations) do
      inc.locations:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, 0, %d},\n", u(loc.kind), u(loc.value_type), u(loc.reg), u(loc.index)))
      counts.locations = counts.locations + 1
    end
    for _, ep in ipairs(endpoints) do
      local ep_loc_start = u(ep.location_start)
      if ep_loc_start > 0 then ep_loc_start = location_start + ep_loc_start - 1 end
      local ep_proj_start = u(ep.projection_start)
      if ep_proj_start > 0 then ep_proj_start = projection_start + ep_proj_start - 1 end
      inc.endpoints:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d},\n", u(ep.kind), u(ep.flags), ep_loc_start, u(ep.n_locations), 0, ep_proj_start, u(ep.n_projections)))
      counts.endpoints = counts.endpoints + 1
    end
    for _, r in ipairs(data_relocs) do
      inc.data_relocs:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d},\n", u(r.code_offset), u(r.reloc_kind), u(r.role_kind), u(r.op_idx), u(r.role_arg), u(r.addend)))
      counts.data_relocs = counts.data_relocs + 1
    end
    for _, r in ipairs(control_relocs) do
      inc.control_relocs:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d},\n", u(r.code_offset), u(r.reloc_kind), u(r.edge_kind), u(r.endpoint_index), u(r.target_delta)))
      counts.control_relocs = counts.control_relocs + 1
    end
    for _, sm in ipairs(slotmaps) do
      inc.slotmaps:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d},\n", u(sm.op_idx), u(sm.logical_slot), u(sm.field_kind)))
      counts.slotmaps = counts.slotmaps + 1
    end
    for _, p in ipairs(projections) do
      inc.projections:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d},\n", u(p.kind), u(p.value_type), u(p.logical_slot), u(p.value_index), u(p.pc)))
      counts.projections = counts.projections + 1
    end
    for _, d in ipairs(dependencies) do
      inc.dependencies:write(string.format("  {%s},\n", c_string(d.name or d[1] or "")))
      counts.dependencies = counts.dependencies + 1
    end

    counts.stencils = counts.stencils + 1
    if counts.stencils % 10000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] shard %d stencils=%d\n", shard_index, counts.stencils)) end
  end
  for _, f in pairs(inc) do f:close() end

  local c_path = string.format("%s/%s.c", out_dir, prefix)
  local c = assert(io.open(c_path, "wb"))
  c:write("/* Generated by src/build_bank.lua. Native stencil bank shard. */\n")
  c:write('#include "sponbank.h"\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n\n')
  write_array_header(c, "SponStencilDesc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_stencils", shard_index), nil)
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "stencils")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "unsigned char", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_data", shard_index), "0,")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "data")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponEndpointDesc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_endpoints", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "endpoints")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponLocationDesc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_locations", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "locations")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponDataReloc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_data_relocs", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "data_relocs")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponControlReloc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_control_relocs", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "control_relocs")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponSlotMapEntry", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_slotmaps", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "slotmaps")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponProjectionEntry", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_projections", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "projections")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponDependencyDesc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_dependencies", shard_index), "{NULL},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "dependencies")); write_array_footer(c)
  c:close()

  return {
    chunk = shard_index,
    first = first_id,
    count = counts.stencils,
    bytes = counts.bytes,
    endpoints = counts.endpoints,
    locations = counts.locations,
    data_relocs = counts.data_relocs,
    control_relocs = counts.control_relocs,
    slotmaps = counts.slotmaps,
    projections = counts.projections,
    dependencies = counts.dependencies,
    c_file = Util.basename(c_path),
  }
end

local function add_pattern_candidate(patterns, l0, ops, stencil_id, selector_sig)
  local pattern_num = selector_key_num(ops)
  local score = sig_pop(selector_sig)
  local list = patterns[pattern_num]
  if not list then list = {}; patterns[pattern_num] = list end
  list[#list + 1] = score * U32 + stencil_id

  if #(ops or {}) == 1 then
    local opcode = OPCODE[op_name(ops[1])] or nil
    if opcode and opcode >= 0 and opcode < 256 then
      if selector_sig == "0x0000000000000000ULL" or not l0[opcode] then l0[opcode] = stencil_id end
    end
  end
end

local function load_pattern_aliases(db, patterns, l0)
  local n = 0
  local sql = [[SELECT u.bank_id AS id, u.selector_sig AS selector_sig, p.ops_json AS ops_json
    FROM unique_forms u JOIN form_patterns p ON p.dedupe_key = u.dedupe_key
    WHERE u.lower_ok = 1 AND u.stencil_json IS NOT NULL AND u.bank_id IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY p.ops_key, u.id]]
  for row in db:nrows(sql) do
    add_pattern_candidate(patterns, l0, assert(cjson.decode(row.ops_json)), tonumber(row.id), as_c_u64_literal(row.selector_sig))
    n = n + 1
    if n % 100000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] loaded %d pattern aliases\n", n)) end
  end
  return n
end

function M.build(bank_src, out_dir)
  bank_src = bank_src or "build/stencil_bank"
  out_dir = out_dir or "build/sponbank"
  mkdir_p(out_dir)

  local db_path = bank_src
  if not tostring(db_path):match("%.sqlite$") then db_path = bank_src .. "/stencil_bank.sqlite" end
  assert(Util.path_exists(db_path), "missing SQLite stencil bank: " .. db_path)
  local db = assert(sqlite3.open(db_path))

  local total_stencils, max_id = 0, 0
  for row in db:nrows("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, COALESCE(MAX(bank_id), 0) AS max_id FROM unique_forms WHERE lower_ok = 1 AND stencil_json IS NOT NULL AND bank_id IS NOT NULL") do
    total_stencils = tonumber(row.n) or 0
    max_id = tonumber(row.max_id) or 0
  end
  assert(total_stencils > 0, "stencil_bank.sqlite has no lowered stencil rows")

  local rows_per_shard = tonumber(os.getenv("BANK_ROWS_PER_SHARD") or "50000") or 50000
  local patterns = {}
  local l0 = {}
  local shards = {}
  local t0 = os.time()
  local shard_index = 1
  local first_id = 1
  while first_id <= max_id do
    local last_id = math.min(max_id, first_id + rows_per_shard - 1)
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] generating shard %d ids=%d..%d\n", shard_index, first_id, last_id))
    local shard = emit_sqlite_shard(db, out_dir, shard_index, first_id, last_id, patterns, l0)
    if shard.count > 0 then shards[#shards + 1] = shard end
    first_id = last_id + 1
    shard_index = shard_index + 1
  end
  table.sort(shards, function(a, b) return a.first < b.first end)

  io.stderr:write("[bank] loading pattern aliases\n")
  local pattern_aliases = load_pattern_aliases(db, patterns, l0)
  db:close()

  io.stderr:write("[bank] generating index/selector\n")
  local index = emit_index(out_dir, shards, patterns, l0, total_stencils)

  local summary = {
    kind = "sponjit.c_stencil_bank_build.v1",
    source = db_path,
    output = out_dir,
    stencils = total_stencils,
    max_id = max_id,
    rows_per_shard = rows_per_shard,
    chunks = #shards,
    patterns = index.patterns,
    candidates = index.candidates,
    pattern_aliases = pattern_aliases,
    seconds = os.time() - t0,
    shards = shards,
    index = index,
  }
  assert(Util.write_json(out_dir .. "/sponbank_build_manifest.json", summary))
  io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] stencils=%d patterns=%d candidates=%d shards=%d done in %ds\n", summary.stencils, summary.patterns, summary.candidates, summary.chunks, summary.seconds))
  return summary
end

if arg and arg[0] and tostring(arg[0]):match("build_bank%.lua$") then
  M.build(arg[1] or "build/stencil_bank", arg[2] or "build/sponbank")
end

return M

-- dedupe_normal_forms.lua -- Global foundry normal-form merge + unique lowering.
--
-- This is the semantic boundary that the monolithic foundry implies:
--   opcode sequence + facts -> SSA consumes semantics -> canonical normal form
--   -> global equivalence class -> lower one representative to the descriptor bank.
--
-- Per-worker DBs contain normal forms and pattern aliases. This script merges
-- equivalent forms across workers, lowers each unique final form once, and keeps
-- all source-pattern aliases so the C selector can map many opcode sequences to
-- the same unique bank id.

package.path = 'src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;' .. package.path

local cjson = require("cjson")
local sqlite3 = require("lsqlite3")
local Util = require("src.util")
local StencilLower = require("src.stencil_lower")
local StencilDesc = require("src.stencil_desc")

local tmpdir = arg[1] or os.getenv("SPON_TMP") or "build/stencil_bank"
local chunks = tonumber(arg[2] or os.getenv("CHUNKS") or "128") or 128
local out_path = arg[3] or (tmpdir .. "/stencil_bank.sqlite")

local function worker_path(ci) return tmpdir .. "/grammar_worker_" .. ci .. ".sqlite" end

local function step_done_for(db, stmt)
  local rc = stmt:step()
  if rc ~= sqlite3.DONE then error(db:errmsg()) end
  stmt:reset()
end

local function numeric_key_map(t)
  if type(t) ~= "table" then return t end
  local out = {}
  for k, v in pairs(t) do out[tonumber(k) or k] = v end
  return out
end

local function rehydrate_ssa_payload(p)
  p = p or {}
  local st = p.stencil or {}
  st.slot_class_by_concrete = numeric_key_map(st.slot_class_by_concrete)
  st.slot_concrete_by_class = st.slot_concrete_by_class or {}
  p.stencil = st
  return p
end

os.remove(out_path)
local db = assert(sqlite3.open(out_path))
assert(db:exec([[
PRAGMA journal_mode = OFF;
PRAGMA synchronous = OFF;
PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY;
PRAGMA locking_mode = EXCLUSIVE;
CREATE TABLE unique_forms (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  stencil_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  contract_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  selector_sig TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  first_chunk INTEGER NOT NULL,
  form_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  ssa_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  lower_ok INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  bank_id INTEGER UNIQUE,
  stencil_json TEXT,
  lower_errors_json TEXT
);
CREATE TABLE form_patterns (
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY(dedupe_key, ops_key)
);
CREATE TABLE chunk_stats (
  chunk INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  seqs INTEGER NOT NULL,
  compiles INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ok INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ssa_ok INTEGER NOT NULL,
  rejected INTEGER NOT NULL,
  forms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  stencils INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE bank_stats (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value INTEGER NOT NULL
);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local insert_unique = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO unique_forms
  (dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, first_chunk, form_json, ssa_json)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))
local insert_pattern = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO form_patterns
  (dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key, ops_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))
local insert_chunk = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO chunk_stats
  (chunk, seqs, compiles, ok, ssa_ok, rejected, forms, stencils)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)]]))

local totals = {seqs = 0, compiles = 0, ok = 0, ssa_ok = 0, rejected = 0, forms = 0, stencils = 0, patterns = 0}

for ci = 1, chunks do
  local path = worker_path(ci)
  assert(Util.path_exists(path), "missing worker db: " .. path)
  local w = assert(sqlite3.open(path))
  local stats = {}
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT key, value FROM stats") do stats[row.key] = tonumber(row.value) or 0 end
  insert_chunk:bind_values(ci, stats.seqs or 0, stats.compiles or 0, stats.ok or 0, stats.ssa_ok or 0, stats.rejected or 0, stats.forms or 0, stats.stencils or 0)
  step_done_for(db, insert_chunk)
  totals.seqs = totals.seqs + (stats.seqs or 0)
  totals.compiles = totals.compiles + (stats.compiles or 0)
  totals.ok = totals.ok + (stats.ok or 0)
  totals.ssa_ok = totals.ssa_ok + (stats.ssa_ok or 0)
  totals.rejected = totals.rejected + (stats.rejected or 0)
  totals.forms = totals.forms + (stats.forms or 0)
  totals.stencils = totals.stencils + (stats.stencils or 0)

  local n = 0
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, form_json, ssa_json FROM forms ORDER BY rowid") do
    insert_unique:bind_values(row.dedupe_key, row.stencil_hash, row.contract_key, row.selector_sig, tonumber(row.count) or 1, ci, row.form_json, row.ssa_json)
    step_done_for(db, insert_unique)
    n = n + 1
    if n % 50000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] chunk %d merged %d forms\n", ci, n)) end
  end

  local pn = 0
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count FROM patterns ORDER BY rowid") do
    insert_pattern:bind_values(row.dedupe_key, row.ops_key, row.ops_json, tonumber(row.count) or 1)
    step_done_for(db, insert_pattern)
    pn = pn + 1
  end
  totals.patterns = totals.patterns + pn
  w:close()
  io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] chunk %d/%d forms=%d patterns=%d compiles=%d\n", ci, chunks, stats.forms or 0, pn, stats.compiles or 0))
end

insert_unique:finalize()
insert_pattern:finalize()
insert_chunk:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT;") == sqlite3.OK)

assert(db:exec([[
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_stencil_hash_idx ON unique_forms(stencil_hash);
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_count_idx ON unique_forms(count);
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_bank_id_idx ON unique_forms(bank_id);
CREATE INDEX form_patterns_ops_key_idx ON form_patterns(ops_key);
CREATE INDEX form_patterns_dedupe_key_idx ON form_patterns(dedupe_key);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local update_lower = assert(db:prepare([[UPDATE unique_forms
  SET lower_ok = ?, bank_id = ?, stencil_json = ?, lower_errors_json = ? WHERE id = ?]]))
local unique_forms, lowered_stencils, lower_rejected = 0, 0, 0
for row in db:nrows("SELECT id, form_json, ssa_json FROM unique_forms ORDER BY id") do
  unique_forms = unique_forms + 1
  local id = tonumber(row.id)
  local form = assert(cjson.decode(row.form_json))
  local ssa = rehydrate_ssa_payload(assert(cjson.decode(row.ssa_json)))
  local lower = StencilLower.generate(ssa, { facts = form.facts or ssa.facts or {} })
  if lower.ok then
    local stencil = lower.stencil
    lowered_stencils = lowered_stencils + 1
    stencil.stencil_id = lowered_stencils
    stencil.abi = StencilDesc.lower_to_abi(stencil)
    update_lower:bind_values(1, lowered_stencils, cjson.encode(stencil), nil, id)
  else
    update_lower:bind_values(0, nil, nil, cjson.encode(lower.errors or {}), id)
    lower_rejected = lower_rejected + 1
  end
  step_done_for(db, update_lower)
  if unique_forms % 50000 == 0 then
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] lowered %d unique forms, stencils=%d rejected=%d\n", unique_forms, lowered_stencils, lower_rejected))
  end
end
update_lower:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT;") == sqlite3.OK)

local pattern_aliases = 0
for row in db:nrows("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM form_patterns") do pattern_aliases = tonumber(row.n) or 0 end

local stat_stmt = assert(db:prepare("INSERT INTO bank_stats(key, value) VALUES (?, ?)"))
local function stat(k, v)
  stat_stmt:bind_values(k, v or 0)
  step_done_for(db, stat_stmt)
end
stat("chunks", chunks)
stat("seqs", totals.seqs)
stat("compiles", totals.compiles)
stat("ok", totals.ok)
stat("ssa_ok", totals.ssa_ok)
stat("ssa_rejected", totals.rejected)
stat("worker_unique_forms", totals.forms)
stat("unique_forms", unique_forms)
stat("lowered_stencils", lowered_stencils)
stat("lower_rejected", lower_rejected)
stat("pattern_aliases", pattern_aliases)
stat_stmt:finalize()
db:close()

io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] DONE chunks=%d compiles=%d worker_forms=%d unique_forms=%d lowered_stencils=%d pattern_aliases=%d db=%s\n",
  chunks, totals.compiles, totals.forms, unique_forms, lowered_stencils, pattern_aliases, out_path))

-- worker_compile.lua — One parallel worker: SSA compile + stencil normal forms.
-- Usage: luajit src/worker_compile.lua <chunk_id>
--
-- Workers run the foundry semantic pass only:
--   opcode sequence + facts -> SSA -> canonical normal form.
-- They do not lower descriptors. Global unique final forms are merged and lowered
-- once by dedupe_normal_forms.lua before C bank generation.

package.path = 'src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;' .. package.path

local cjson = require("cjson")
local sqlite3 = require("lsqlite3")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local Contract = require("src.ssa_contract")
local Util = require("src.util")
local FactAxes = require("src.ssa_fact_axes")

local ci = tonumber(arg[1])
assert(ci, "usage: luajit src/worker_compile.lua <chunk_id>")

local config = {
  max_fact_combos = tonumber(os.getenv("MAX_FACT_COMBOS") or "0") or 0,
  fact_axis_mode = tostring(os.getenv("FACT_AXIS_MODE") or "curated"),
}
local progress_every = tonumber(os.getenv("WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS") or "1000") or 1000

local tmpdir = os.getenv("SPON_TMP") or "/tmp"
local function tmp(name) return tmpdir .. "/" .. name end
local seqs = Util.read_json(tmp("grammar_chunk_" .. ci .. ".json"))

io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] START seqs=%d fact_mode=%s max_fact_combos=%s\n",
  ci, #seqs, config.fact_axis_mode, tostring(config.max_fact_combos)))

local function op_signature(ops)
  local parts = {}
  for _, op in ipairs(ops or {}) do parts[#parts + 1] = type(op) == "table" and tostring(op.op) or tostring(op) end
  return table.concat(parts, "|")
end

local function contract_key(contract)
  return table.concat({
    tostring(contract.selector_sig and contract.selector_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.required_sig and contract.required_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.checked_sig and contract.checked_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.produced_sig and contract.produced_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.killed_sig and contract.killed_sig.literal or ""),
  }, "|")
end

local db_path = tmp("grammar_worker_" .. ci .. ".sqlite")
os.remove(db_path)
local db = assert(sqlite3.open(db_path))
assert(db:exec([[
PRAGMA journal_mode = OFF;
PRAGMA synchronous = OFF;
PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY;
PRAGMA locking_mode = EXCLUSIVE;
CREATE TABLE forms (
  dedupe_key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  stencil_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  contract_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  selector_sig TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  form_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  ssa_json TEXT NOT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE patterns (
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY(dedupe_key, ops_key)
);
CREATE TABLE stats (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value INTEGER NOT NULL
);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local insert_form = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO forms
  (dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, form_json, ssa_json)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)]]))
local bump_form = assert(db:prepare([[UPDATE forms SET count = count + 1 WHERE dedupe_key = ?]]))
local insert_pattern = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO patterns
  (dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key, ops_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))

local function step_done(stmt)
  local rc = stmt:step()
  if rc ~= sqlite3.DONE then error(db:errmsg()) end
  stmt:reset()
end

local forms_seen = {}
local lc, lssa_ok, nforms = 0, 0, 0

for si, ops in ipairs(seqs) do
  local subsets = FactAxes.subsets(FactAxes.axes_for_ops(ops), config)
  for _, facts in ipairs(subsets) do
    local r = SSA.compile(ops, facts, config)
    lc = lc + 1
    if r.ok then
      lssa_ok = lssa_ok + 1
      local contract = Contract.from_result(r, facts)
      local ckey = contract_key(contract)
      local normal_key = tostring(r.stencil_hash)
      -- Equivalence is the SSA-consumed canonical normal form. The contract is
      -- retained as descriptor metadata for the chosen representative, but it is
      -- not part of the dedupe key: source facts/opcodes are aliases of the form.
      local dedupe_key = normal_key
      local ops_key = op_signature(ops)

      insert_pattern:bind_values(dedupe_key, ops_key, cjson.encode(ops), 1)
      step_done(insert_pattern)

      if forms_seen[dedupe_key] then
        bump_form:bind_values(dedupe_key)
        step_done(bump_form)
      else
        nforms = nforms + 1
        forms_seen[dedupe_key] = true
        local form = {
          key = normal_key,
          dedupe_key = dedupe_key,
          stencil_hash = r.stencil_hash,
          stencil_form = r.stencil_form,
          stencil_key = r.stencil_key,
          stencil_ops = r.stencil_ops,
          stencil_slotmaps = r.slotmaps,
          ops = ops,
          facts = facts,
          contract = contract,
          count = 1,
          changed = r.changed,
          source_ops = ops,
        }
        local ssa_payload = {
          stencil = r.stencil,
          stencil_hash = r.stencil_hash,
          stencil_key = r.stencil_key,
          stencil_ops = r.stencil_ops,
          slotmaps = r.slotmaps,
          active_node_specs = r.active_node_specs,
          checked_facts = r.checked_facts,
          deps = r.deps,
          projection = r.projection,
          source_ops = ops,
          facts = facts,
        }
        local selector_sig = tostring(contract.selector_sig and contract.selector_sig.literal or "0x0000000000000000ULL")
        insert_form:bind_values(dedupe_key, normal_key, ckey, selector_sig, 1, cjson.encode(form), cjson.encode(ssa_payload))
        step_done(insert_form)
      end
    end
  end
  if progress_every > 0 and (si % progress_every == 0 or si == #seqs) then
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] %d/%d compiles=%d ssa_ok=%d unique_forms=%d\n", ci, si, #seqs, lc, lssa_ok, nforms))
  end
end

insert_form:finalize()
bump_form:finalize()
insert_pattern:finalize()
local stats = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO stats(key, value) VALUES (?, ?)]]))
local ssa_rejected = lc - lssa_ok
for _, kv in ipairs({
  {"seqs", #seqs},
  {"compiles", lc},
  {"ok", lssa_ok},
  {"ssa_ok", lssa_ok},
  {"rejected", ssa_rejected},
  {"forms", nforms},
  {"stencils", 0},
}) do
  stats:bind_values(kv[1], kv[2])
  step_done(stats)
end
stats:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT; CREATE INDEX forms_stencil_hash_idx ON forms(stencil_hash); CREATE INDEX patterns_ops_key_idx ON patterns(ops_key);") == sqlite3.OK)
db:close()

io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] DONE seqs=%d compiles=%d ssa_ok=%d rejected=%d unique_forms=%d db=%s\n", ci, #seqs, lc, lssa_ok, ssa_rejected, nforms, db_path))

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# build_stencils.sh — Chunked SpongeJIT foundry metadata build.
#
# Pipeline:
#   grammar enumeration -> weighted JSON chunks -> parallel SSA workers
#   -> per-worker SQLite normal-form DBs -> global normal-form dedupe
#   -> lower each unique supported final form once.
#
# This is a chunked replacement for the monolithic foundry pass. The semantic
# key is the foundry key: the SSA-consumed canonical Stencil IR hash.
# Source opcode sequences and fact contracts are aliases, not the equivalence key.
# Output: build/stencil_bank/stencil_bank.sqlite for src/build_bank.lua.

set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")"

if command -v luarocks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  eval "$(luarocks --lua-version=5.1 path 2>/dev/null || luarocks path)"
fi
export LUA_PATH LUA_CPATH PATH

log() { printf '[stencils] %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

CORES=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 16)
CHUNKS=${CHUNKS:-128}
WORKERS=${WORKERS:-$CORES}
MAX_ARITY=${MAX_ARITY:-4}
MAX_FACT_COMBOS=${MAX_FACT_COMBOS:-32}
FACT_AXIS_MODE=${FACT_AXIS_MODE:-curated}
WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS=${WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS:-1000}
OUT_DIR=${OUT_DIR:-"$PWD/build/stencil_bank"}
SPON_TMP=${SPON_TMP:-"$OUT_DIR"}
KEEP_STENCIL_PARTIALS=${KEEP_STENCIL_PARTIALS:-0}

export CHUNKS WORKERS MAX_ARITY MAX_FACT_COMBOS FACT_AXIS_MODE WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS SPON_TMP

mkdir -p "$SPON_TMP"

if [ "$KEEP_STENCIL_PARTIALS" != 1 ]; then
  rm -f "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_chunk_*.json \
        "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_chunk_manifest.json \
        "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_worker_*.sqlite \
        "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_forms_*.jsonl \
        "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_result_*.json \
        "$SPON_TMP"/stencil_bank.sqlite \
        "$SPON_TMP"/stencil_bank_manifest.json \
        "$SPON_TMP"/worker_*.log \
        "$SPON_TMP"/build_config.env
fi

cat > "$SPON_TMP/build_config.env" <<EOF
CHUNKS=$CHUNKS
WORKERS=$WORKERS
MAX_ARITY=$MAX_ARITY
MAX_FACT_COMBOS=$MAX_FACT_COMBOS
FACT_AXIS_MODE=$FACT_AXIS_MODE
WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS=$WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS
SPON_TMP=$SPON_TMP
EOF

log "config: chunks=$CHUNKS workers=$WORKERS max_arity=$MAX_ARITY fact_mode=$FACT_AXIS_MODE max_fact_combos=$MAX_FACT_COMBOS out=$SPON_TMP"

log "1/3 grammar enumeration + weighted chunk plan"
luajit -e '
package.path = "src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;" .. package.path
local Util = require("src.util")
local Grammar = require("src.grammar_enum")
local FactAxes = require("src.ssa_fact_axes")

local max_arity = tonumber(os.getenv("MAX_ARITY") or "4") or 4
local chunks = tonumber(os.getenv("CHUNKS") or "128") or 128
local config = {
  max_fact_combos = tonumber(os.getenv("MAX_FACT_COMBOS") or "32") or 32,
  fact_axis_mode = tostring(os.getenv("FACT_AXIS_MODE") or "curated"),
}
local tmp = assert(os.getenv("SPON_TMP"), "SPON_TMP unset")
local function t(name) return tmp .. "/" .. name end

local seqs = Grammar.generate_all(max_arity)

-- generate_all() is handler-deduped. Add exact L0 floor forms for every
-- concrete opcode so the bank keeps total floor coverage explicit.
local seen_l0 = {}
for _, seq in ipairs(seqs) do
  if #(seq.ops or {}) == 1 then
    local op = seq.ops[1]
    local name = type(op) == "table" and op.op or tostring(op)
    seen_l0[name] = true
  end
end
for _, seq in ipairs(Grammar.generate_l0_all()) do
  local op = seq.ops[1]
  local name = type(op) == "table" and op.op or tostring(op)
  if not seen_l0[name] then
    seqs[#seqs + 1] = seq
    seen_l0[name] = true
  end
end

local function estimate_combos(ops)
  local axes = FactAxes.axes_for_ops(ops)
  local subsets = FactAxes.subsets(axes, config)
  return math.max(1, #subsets)
end

local items, total_cost, max_cost = {}, 0, 0
for i, seq in ipairs(seqs) do
  local combos = estimate_combos(seq.ops)
  items[#items + 1] = {idx = i, ops = seq.ops, cost = combos}
  total_cost = total_cost + combos
  if combos > max_cost then max_cost = combos end
  if i % 50000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[plan] costed %d/%d sequences\n", i, #seqs)) end
end

table.sort(items, function(a, b)
  if a.cost ~= b.cost then return a.cost > b.cost end
  return a.idx < b.idx
end)

local buckets = {}
for ci = 1, chunks do buckets[ci] = {cost = 0, count = 0, ops = {}} end
for _, item in ipairs(items) do
  local best = 1
  for ci = 2, chunks do if buckets[ci].cost < buckets[best].cost then best = ci end end
  local b = buckets[best]
  b.cost = b.cost + item.cost
  b.count = b.count + 1
  b.ops[#b.ops + 1] = item.ops
end

local min_cost, max_bucket_cost = math.huge, 0
local manifest = {
  max_arity = max_arity,
  chunks = chunks,
  sequences = #seqs,
  estimated_compiles = total_cost,
  max_sequence_combos = max_cost,
  fact_axis_mode = config.fact_axis_mode,
  max_fact_combos = config.max_fact_combos,
  buckets = {},
}
for ci = 1, chunks do
  local b = buckets[ci]
  if b.cost < min_cost then min_cost = b.cost end
  if b.cost > max_bucket_cost then max_bucket_cost = b.cost end
  Util.write_json(t("grammar_chunk_" .. ci .. ".json"), b.ops)
  manifest.buckets[#manifest.buckets + 1] = {chunk = ci, sequences = b.count, estimated_compiles = b.cost}
end
Util.write_json(t("grammar_chunk_manifest.json"), manifest)
io.stderr:write(string.format("[plan] %d seqs incl exact L0, arity<=%d, %d weighted chunks, est_compiles=%d, per_chunk=%d..%d, max_seq_combos=%d\n",
  #seqs, max_arity, chunks, total_cost, min_cost == math.huge and 0 or min_cost, max_bucket_cost, max_cost))
'

log "2/3 SSA + per-worker SQLite ($WORKERS workers over $CHUNKS chunks)"
seq 1 "$CHUNKS" | xargs -P "$WORKERS" -I{} sh -c '
  set -e
  ci="$1"
  log="$SPON_TMP/worker_${ci}.log"
  echo "[worker ${ci}] START" >&2
  luajit src/worker_compile.lua "$ci" >"$log" 2>&1
  tail -n 3 "$log" >&2 || true
  echo "[worker ${ci}] DONE log=$log" >&2
' sh {}

log "3/3 global normal-form dedupe + unique lowering"
luajit src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua "$SPON_TMP" "$CHUNKS" "$SPON_TMP/stencil_bank.sqlite" 2>&1

log "done: $SPON_TMP/stencil_bank.sqlite"

#!/usr/bin/env luajit

package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;" .. package.path

local ffi = require("ffi")
local Facts = require("src.facts")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local StencilLower = require("src.stencil_lower")

ffi.cdef[[
typedef unsigned long size_t;
typedef struct SponTValueABI {
  unsigned long long value_;
  unsigned char tt_;
} SponTValueABI;
typedef struct SponExecCtx {
  void *stack;
  SponTValueABI *k;
  SponTValueABI scratch[256];
  uint32_t exit_kind;
  uint32_t exit_pc;
  uint32_t exit_op_idx;
  uint32_t exit_hole;
} SponExecCtx;
void *mmap(void *addr, size_t length, int prot, int flags, int fd, long offset);
int munmap(void *addr, size_t length);
void *memcpy(void *dest, const void *src, size_t n);
]]

local function assert_eq(a, b, msg) if a ~= b then error((msg or "assert_eq failed") .. ": " .. tostring(a) .. " ~= " .. tostring(b), 2) end end
local function assert_true(x, msg) if not x then error(msg or "assert_true failed", 2) end end

local function hex_to_buf(hex)
  local n = #hex / 2
  local buf = ffi.new("uint8_t[?]", n)
  local i = 0
  for pair in hex:gmatch("%x%x") do buf[i] = tonumber(pair, 16); i = i + 1 end
  return buf, n
end

local function write_i64(buf, off, v)
  local n = tonumber(v)
  for i = 0, 7 do
    buf[off + i] = n % 256
    n = math.floor(n / 256)
  end
end

local function executable_copy(buf, n)
  local PROT_READ, PROT_WRITE, PROT_EXEC = 1, 2, 4
  local MAP_PRIVATE, MAP_ANON = 2, 0x20
  local mem = ffi.C.mmap(nil, n, PROT_READ + PROT_WRITE + PROT_EXEC, MAP_PRIVATE + MAP_ANON, -1, 0)
  assert_true(mem ~= ffi.cast("void *", -1), "mmap failed")
  ffi.C.memcpy(mem, buf, n)
  return mem
end

local function lower_native(ops, facts)
  local r = SSA.compile(ops, facts or {})
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, { facts = facts or {} })
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_true(fr.stencil.executable == true and (fr.stencil.code_size or 0) > 0, "native stencil expected")
  return fr.stencil
end

local function run_native(f, patch, setup, check)
  local buf, n = hex_to_buf(f.code_hex)
  if patch then patch(buf, f) end
  local mem = executable_copy(buf, n)
  local fn = ffi.cast("void (*)(SponExecCtx *)", mem)
  local stack = ffi.new("SponTValueABI[8]")
  local k = ffi.new("SponTValueABI[4]")
  local ctx = ffi.new("SponExecCtx")
  ctx.stack = stack
  ctx.k = k
  if setup then setup(ctx, stack, k) end
  fn(ctx)
  if check then check(ctx, stack, k) end
  ffi.C.munmap(mem, n)
end

assert_eq(ffi.sizeof("SponTValueABI"), 16, "TValue ABI size")
assert_eq(ffi.offsetof("SponExecCtx", "exit_kind"), 16 + 256 * 16, "ctx exit offset")

local facts = { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true) }
local f = lower_native({ { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 7 } }, facts)
assert_true(f.executable == true, "stencil must be executable")
assert_true((f.code_size or 0) > 0 and type(f.code_hex) == "string", "native code bytes missing")
assert_eq(f.abi.stencil.flags, 2, "native flag")

local buf, n = hex_to_buf(f.code_hex)
local saw_abs64, saw_pc32, saw_slot_addend8 = false, false, false
for _, dr in ipairs(f.data_relocs or {}) do
  assert_true(dr.code_offset_kind ~= "abstract_zero", "data reloc must be real")
  if dr.reloc_kind == "abs64" and dr.role_kind == "imm" then write_i64(buf, dr.code_offset, 7); saw_abs64 = true end
  if dr.role_kind == "slot" and dr.addend == 8 then saw_slot_addend8 = true end
end
for _, cr in ipairs(f.control_relocs or {}) do
  assert_eq(cr.reloc_kind, "pc32", "control reloc must be rel32")
  saw_pc32 = true
end
assert_true(saw_abs64, "imm64 patch site missing")
assert_true(saw_pc32, "guard control patch site missing")
assert_true(saw_slot_addend8, "slot tag displacement patch site missing")

local mem = executable_copy(buf, n)
local fn = ffi.cast("void (*)(SponExecCtx *)", mem)
local stack = ffi.new("SponTValueABI[8]")
local ctx = ffi.new("SponExecCtx")
ctx.stack = stack

stack[0].value_ = 35
stack[0].tt_ = 3 -- LUA_VNUMINT
ctx.exit_kind = 99
fn(ctx)
assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "success exit kind")
assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].value_), 42, "ADDI native result")
assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].tt_), 3, "ADDI native result tag")

stack[0].value_ = 35
stack[0].tt_ = 0 -- not integer; guard must branch to local guard endpoint stub
ctx.exit_kind = 99
fn(ctx)
assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 1, "guard exit kind")
assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_pc), 1, "guard exit pc")

ffi.C.munmap(mem, n)

run_native(lower_native({ { op = "LOADTRUE", a = 0 } }), nil,
  function(ctx, stack) stack[0].value_ = 0; stack[0].tt_ = 0; ctx.exit_kind = 99 end,
  function(ctx, stack) assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "LOADTRUE exit"); assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].tt_), 17, "LOADTRUE tag") end)

run_native(lower_native({ { op = "LOADNIL", a = 0 } }), nil,
  function(ctx, stack) stack[0].value_ = 123; stack[0].tt_ = 3; ctx.exit_kind = 99 end,
  function(ctx, stack) assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "LOADNIL exit"); assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].tt_), 0, "LOADNIL tag") end)

run_native(lower_native({ { op = "MOVE", a = 0, b = 1 } }), nil,
  function(ctx, stack) stack[1].value_ = 12345; stack[1].tt_ = 3; ctx.exit_kind = 99 end,
  function(ctx, stack) assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "MOVE exit"); assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].value_), 12345, "MOVE value"); assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].tt_), 3, "MOVE tag") end)

run_native(lower_native({ { op = "LOADK", a = 0, b = 0 } }), nil,
  function(ctx, stack, k) k[0].value_ = 55; k[0].tt_ = 3; ctx.exit_kind = 99 end,
  function(ctx, stack) assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "LOADK exit"); assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].value_), 55, "LOADK value"); assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].tt_), 3, "LOADK tag") end)

print("ok - SpongeJIT native stencil bytes execute")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit

package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;" .. package.path

local Facts = require("src.facts")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local StencilLower = require("src.stencil_lower")

local function assert_eq(a, b, msg) if a ~= b then error((msg or "assert_eq failed") .. ": " .. tostring(a) .. " ~= " .. tostring(b), 2) end end
local function assert_true(x, msg) if not x then error(msg or "assert_true failed", 2) end end
local function assert_false(x, msg) if x then error(msg or "assert_false failed", 2) end end

local function has_endpoint(f, kind)
  for _, ep in ipairs(f.endpoints or {}) do if ep.kind == kind then return true, ep end end
  return false
end

local function has_data_role(f, role)
  for _, r in ipairs(f.data_relocs or {}) do if r.role_kind == role then return true end end
  return false
end

local function has_control(f, kind)
  for _, r in ipairs(f.control_relocs or {}) do if r.edge_kind == kind then return true, r end end
  return false
end

local function has_error(errors, needle)
  for _, e in ipairs(errors or {}) do if tostring(e):find(needle, 1, true) then return true end end
  return false
end

local function assert_exit_projection(f, endpoint_kind, control_kind)
  local ok_ep, ep = has_endpoint(f, endpoint_kind)
  assert_true(ok_ep, endpoint_kind .. " endpoint missing")
  assert_true(ep.projection_start and f.projections[ep.projection_start], endpoint_kind .. " projection range missing")
  assert_true((ep.n_projections or 0) > 0, endpoint_kind .. " projection count missing")
  assert_true(has_control(f, control_kind), control_kind .. " control reloc missing")
end

-- End-to-end conforming i64 slot arithmetic stencil descriptor.
do
  local facts = { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true) }
  local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, facts)
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, { facts = facts })
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local f = fr.stencil
  assert_eq(f.physical_abi, "x86_64_sysv_spon_v1", "physical ABI")
  assert_eq(f.abi.stencil.physical_abi, 1, "ABI-lowered physical ABI")
  assert_eq(f.abi.stencil.flags, 2, "native stencil flag")
  assert_true(f.executable == true and f.layout.mode == "native_bytes", "stencil must carry executable native bytes")
  assert_true(type(f.code_hex) == "string" and #f.code_hex > 0, "native code bytes missing")
  assert_true((f.code_size or 0) > 0 and f.abi.stencil.size == f.code_size, "native code size missing")
  assert_true(has_endpoint(f, "entry"), "entry endpoint missing")
  assert_true(has_endpoint(f, "ok"), "ok endpoint missing")
  assert_true(has_endpoint(f, "guard_exit"), "guard exit endpoint missing")
  for _, ep in ipairs(f.endpoints or {}) do assert_true(#(ep.locations or {}) > 0, "endpoint locations must be explicit") end
  assert_true(#(f.clobbers or {}) > 0, "stencil clobbers must be explicit")
  assert_true(has_data_role(f, "slot"), "slot data reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_data_role(f, "slot_store"), "slot_store data reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_data_role(f, "imm"), "imm data reloc missing")
  assert_true(has_control(f, "guard_fail"), "guard_fail control reloc missing")
  for _, dr in ipairs(f.data_relocs or {}) do
    assert_true(dr.role_kind ~= "fail" and dr.role_kind ~= "exit", "exit/fail must not be data relocs")
    assert_true(dr.code_offset_kind ~= "abstract_zero", "native data reloc must have real offset marker")
    assert_true(dr.reloc_kind == "abs32s" or dr.reloc_kind == "abs64" or dr.reloc_kind == "abs8", "native data reloc encoding missing")
  end
  for _, cr in ipairs(f.control_relocs or {}) do
    assert_true(cr.code_offset_kind ~= "abstract_zero", "native control reloc must have real offset marker")
    assert_eq(cr.reloc_kind, "pc32", "native control reloc encoding")
  end
  assert_true(f.fact_transfer.checked_sig, "checked signature missing")
  assert_true(f.fact_transfer.produced_sig, "produced signature missing")
  assert_true(f.fact_transfer.killed_sig, "killed signature missing")
end

-- Mixed unary/binary integer loop body lowers as one native byte stencil. This
-- keeps the descriptor path from regressing to a metadata-only shape.
do
  local facts = {
    Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_i64", true),
    Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R3"), "is_i64", true),
  }
  local r = SSA.compile({
    { op = "UNM", a = 1, b = 0 },
    { op = "BNOT", a = 2, b = 1 },
    { op = "MUL", a = 3, b = 3, c = 3 },
    { op = "ADDI", a = 4, b = 3, c = 5 },
  }, facts)
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, { facts = facts })
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_eq(fr.stencil.len, 4, "mixed stencil length")
  assert_true(#(fr.stencil.slotmaps or {}) >= 4, "mixed stencil slotmaps")
  assert_true(has_control(fr.stencil, "guard_fail"), "mixed stencil guard control reloc")
end

-- Boundary exit lowering is covered and projected.
do
  local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "RETURN1", a = 1 } }, {})
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, {})
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_exit_projection(fr.stencil, "boundary_exit", "boundary")
end

-- Residual exit lowering is covered and projected.
do
  local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "POW", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, {})
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, {})
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_exit_projection(fr.stencil, "residual_exit", "residual")
end

-- Unlowered semantic node reopening is covered and projected.
do
  local r = SSA.compile_nodes({ { op = "MysteryNativeOp", source = 3, inputs = {}, outputs = {}, effect = "none" } }, {})
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, {})
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_exit_projection(fr.stencil, "unlowered_exit", "boundary")
end

-- Payload/dependency-heavy table/shape stencils reject loudly.
do
  local r = SSA.compile({ "GETFIELD" }, { "table", "shape_known", "metatable_absent", "key_const" })
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, { facts = { "table", "shape_known", "metatable_absent", "key_const" } })
  assert_false(fr.ok, "shape/table stencil must reject unsupported payload/dependency surface")
  assert_true(has_error(fr.errors, "unsupported"), table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
end

print("ok - SpongeJIT stencil to native stencil")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
-- Real SponJIT fact + SSA invariants.

package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;" .. package.path

local Facts = require("src.facts")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local Contract = require("src.ssa_contract")
local Enum = require("src.enumerate")
local FactAxes = require("src.ssa_fact_axes")

local function assert_eq(a, b, msg)
    if a ~= b then error((msg or "assert_eq failed") .. ": " .. tostring(a) .. " ~= " .. tostring(b), 2) end
end

local function assert_true(x, msg)
    if not x then error(msg or "assert_true failed", 2) end
end

local function has(xs, x)
    for _, v in ipairs(xs or {}) do if v == x then return true end end
    return false
end

-- Fact closure: shape_known implies table.
do
    local fs = Facts.new({ "shape_known" })
    assert_true(fs:implies("shape_known", Facts.value("table")), "shape_known absent")
    assert_true(fs:implies("is_table", Facts.value("table")), "shape_known must imply table")
    assert_true(has(fs:deps(), "shape_epoch"), "shape fact must carry shape_epoch dependency")
end

-- Fact contradiction: one subject cannot have two concrete incompatible types.
do
    local fs = Facts.new({
        Facts.fact("type", Facts.value("x"), "is_i64"),
        Facts.fact("type", Facts.value("x"), "is_table"),
    })
    assert_true(not fs:ok(), "conflicting type facts must be rejected")
end

-- Guards are facts with exits and deps.
do
    local r = SSA.compile({ "GETFIELD" }, { "table", "shape_known", "metatable_absent", "key_const" })
    assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_true(has(r.stencil_ops, "guard_shape"), "shape guard missing")
    assert_true(has(r.deps, "shape_epoch"), "shape dep missing")
    assert_true((r.projection.exit_obligations or 0) >= 3, "guards must project exits")
end

-- compile_nodes reopens typed semantic node specs; raw codegen-op reopening was removed.
do
    local base = SSA.compile({ { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true) })
    local saw_mem = false
    local saw_residency = false
    local saw_exit = false
    for _, spec in ipairs(base.active_node_specs or {}) do
        if spec.source ~= nil then assert_true(type(spec.source) == "number", "source must serialize as number") end
        if spec.mem_in and spec.mem_in.frame then saw_mem = true end
        for _, rloc in ipairs(spec.output_residencies or {}) do
            if rloc == "gpr0" then saw_residency = true end
        end
        if spec.exit and spec.exit.reason then saw_exit = true end
    end
    assert_true(saw_mem, "active_node_specs must preserve memory input tokens")
    assert_true(saw_residency, "active_node_specs must preserve value residency")
    assert_true(saw_exit, "active_node_specs must preserve exit objects")

    local r = SSA.compile_nodes(base.active_node_specs, {})
    assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_true(has(r.stencil_ops, "store_i64_slot"), "typed node reopening should preserve lowered integer store")
    local saw_reopened_residency = false
    local saw_reopened_mem_in = false
    local saw_reopened_mem_out = false
    local saw_reopened_exit = false
    for _, spec in ipairs(r.active_node_specs or {}) do
        if spec.mem_in and spec.mem_in.frame then saw_reopened_mem_in = true end
        if spec.mem_out and spec.mem_out.frame then saw_reopened_mem_out = true end
        if spec.exit and spec.exit.reason then saw_reopened_exit = true end
        for _, rloc in ipairs(spec.output_residencies or {}) do
            if rloc == "gpr0" then saw_reopened_residency = true end
        end
    end
    assert_true(saw_reopened_residency, "compile_nodes must round-trip residency")
    assert_true(saw_reopened_mem_in, "compile_nodes must round-trip memory input tokens")
    assert_true(saw_reopened_mem_out, "compile_nodes must round-trip memory output tokens")
    assert_true(saw_reopened_exit, "compile_nodes must round-trip exit objects")
end

-- Barrier elimination requires a real GC fact.
do
    local no_fact = SSA.compile({ "GETFIELD", "SETFIELD" }, { "table", "shape_known", "metatable_absent", "key_const" })
    local with_fact = SSA.compile({ "GETFIELD", "SETFIELD" }, { "table", "shape_known", "metatable_absent", "key_const", "barrier_clean" })
    assert_true(has(no_fact.stencil_ops, "barrier_check"), "barrier should remain without fact")
    assert_true(not has(with_fact.stencil_ops, "barrier_check"), "barrier_clean should remove barrier")
end

-- Operand-specific facts attach to real PUC slots and drive the same optimization.
do
    local ops = {
        { op = "GETFIELD", a = 1, b = 0, c = 2 },
        { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 },
        { op = "SETFIELD", a = 0, b = 2, c = 1 },
    }
    local facts = {
        Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R0"), "is_table"),
        Facts.fact("shape", Facts.slot("R0"), "shape_known", true),
        Facts.fact("metatable", Facts.slot("R0"), "metatable_absent", true),
        Facts.fact("constant", Facts.value("K2"), "key_const", true),
        Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64"),
        Facts.fact("gc", Facts.global_subject(), "barrier_clean", true),
    }
    local r = SSA.compile(ops, facts)
    assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_eq(table.concat(r.stencil_form, "|"), "FIELD_ADDI_UPDATE", "operand-specific field update should fuse semantically")
    assert_true(has(r.checked_facts, "shape_known"), "slot-specific shape fact should be guarded")
end

-- SSA contracts carry real fact lifetime: selector facts checked by tile guards
-- become success-edge facts, writes kill prior slot leases, and stores produce
-- fresh slot facts only when the SSA proves the stored value.
do
    local facts = { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true) }
    local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, facts)
    local c = Contract.from_result(r, facts)
    assert_eq(c.selector_sig.literal, "0x0000000000000001ULL", "selector should include observed canonical S0:i64")
    assert_eq(c.required_sig.literal, "0x0000000000000000ULL", "guarded i64 is checked, not blindly required")
    assert_eq(c.checked_sig.literal, "0x0000000000000001ULL", "ADDI guard establishes canonical S0:i64 on success")
    assert_eq(c.produced_sig.literal, "0x0000000000000001ULL", "ADDI store produces fresh canonical S0:i64")
    assert_true(c.killed_sig.literal ~= "0x0000000000000000ULL", "ADDI store must kill previous R1 leases")
end

-- Enumerator derives typed fact axes from instruction operands.
do
    local axes = Enum.fact_axes_for_ops({ { op = "ADDI", a = 3, b = 2, c = 1 } })
    local found = false
    for _, f in ipairs(axes) do
        if type(f) == "table" and f.predicate == "is_i64" and Facts.subject_key(f.subject) == "slot:R2" then found = true end
    end
    assert_true(found, "ADDI operand B must produce slot:R2 is_i64 fact axis")
end

-- Loop opcodes are structural region boundaries until paired loop-region
-- lowering exists; fact axes must not advertise an i64 loop specialization that
-- no lowering consumes.
do
    local numeric_axes = FactAxes.axes_for_ops({ { op = "FORPREP", a = 0, bx = 3 }, { op = "FORLOOP", a = 0, bx = 3 } })
    assert_eq(#numeric_axes, 0, "FOR* must not produce scalar fact axes before loop-region lowering")
    assert_eq(#(numeric_axes.bundles or {}), 0, "FOR* must not produce loop fact bundles before loop-region lowering")
    assert_eq(#Enum.fact_axes_for_ops({ { op = "FORLOOP", a = 0, bx = 3 } }), 0, "corpus loop facts must not imply native loop lowering")

    local prep = SSA.compile({ { op = "FORPREP", a = 0, bx = 3 } }, {})
    local prep_node = prep.graph:active_nodes()[1]
    assert_eq(prep_node.op, "GenericExit", "FORPREP remains a region boundary")
    assert_eq(prep_node.args.reason, "numeric_for_region_boundary", "FORPREP boundary reason")

    local tfor = SSA.compile({ { op = "TFORCALL", a = 0 } }, {})
    local tfor_node = tfor.graph:active_nodes()[1]
    assert_eq(tfor_node.op, "GenericExit", "TFORCALL remains a region boundary")
    assert_eq(tfor_node.args.reason, "generic_for_region_boundary", "TFORCALL boundary reason")
end

-- Atoms reopen as typed semantic node specs, preserving graph-derived NF.
do
    local r = SSA.compile({ "GETFIELD", "ADDI", "SETFIELD" }, { "table", "shape_known", "metatable_absent", "key_const", "lhs_i64", "barrier_clean" })
    local reopened = SSA.compile_nodes(r.active_node_specs, {})
    assert_true(reopened.ok, table.concat(reopened.errors or {}, "\n"))
    assert_true(has(reopened.stencil_ops, "store_i64_slot"), "atom reopen must preserve lowered store shape")
    assert_true(has(reopened.stencil_ops, "table_field_load"), "atom reopen must preserve field load shape")
end

print("ok - real SponJIT fact + SSA invariants")

{ op = "ADDI", a = 0, b = 0, c = 1 },
    { op = "ADDI", a = 0, b = 0, c = 2 },
    { op = "ADDI", a = 0, b = 0, c = 3 },
    { op = "ADDI", a = 0, b = 0, c = 4 },
    { op = "ADDI", a = 0, b = 0, c = 5 },
  }
  local i64_r0 = 1
  local entries = select_entries(ops, i64_r0, i64_r0)
  assert_true(#entries >= 2, "five-op span should require at least two max-arity choices")
  assert_true(has_edge(entries[1].stencil, 1), "first bank stencil missing fallthrough edge")
  assert_true(has_edge(entries[1].stencil, 2), "first bank stencil missing guard edge")
  local img = Materialize.materialize(entries)
  assert_true(img.ok and img.code_size > 0, "bank materialization failed")

  run_image(img,
    function(ctx, stack)
      stack[0].value_ = 0
      stack[0].tt_ = 3 -- LUA_VNUMINT
      ctx.exit_kind = 99
    end,
    function(ctx, stack)
      assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 0, "bank-linked ADDI exit")
      assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].value_), 15, "bank-linked ADDI result")
      assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].tt_), 3, "bank-linked ADDI tag")
    end)

  run_image(img,
    function(ctx, stack)
      stack[0].value_ = 0
      stack[0].tt_ = 0
      ctx.exit_kind = 99
    end,
    function(ctx, stack)
      assert_eq(tonumber(ctx.exit_kind), 1, "bank-linked guard exit")
      assert_eq(tonumber(stack[0].value_), 0, "guard failure must not run linked span")
    end)
end

print("ok - SpongeJIT generated bank selects and materializes native span")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit

package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;" .. package.path

local StencilDesc = require("src.stencil_desc")
local Abi = require("src.stencil_abi_x64")

local function assert_eq(a, b, msg) if a ~= b then error((msg or "assert_eq failed") .. ": " .. tostring(a) .. " ~= " .. tostring(b), 2) end end
local function assert_true(x, msg) if not x then error(msg or "assert_true failed", 2) end end
local function assert_false(x, msg) if x then error(msg or "assert_false failed", 2) end end
local function has_error(errors, needle)
  for _, e in ipairs(errors or {}) do if tostring(e):find(needle, 1, true) then return true end end
  return false
end

local function fact_transfer()
  return {
    selector_sig = { literal = "0x0ULL" },
    required_sig = { literal = "0x0ULL" },
    checked_sig = { literal = "0x0ULL" },
    produced_sig = { literal = "0x0ULL" },
    killed_sig = { literal = "0x0ULL" },
  }
end

local function locs()
  return {
    StencilDesc.location({ kind = "reg", reg = "rdi", value_type = "Ptr", name = "ctx" }),
    StencilDesc.location({ kind = "ctx_field", index = 0, value_type = "Ptr", name = "ctx.stack.synced_frame" }),
  }
end

local function ep(kind, overrides)
  local e = StencilDesc.endpoint({ kind = kind, locations = locs() })
  for k, v in pairs(overrides or {}) do e[k] = v end
  return e
end

local function minimal(overrides)
  local f = StencilDesc.stencil({
    physical_abi = "x86_64_sysv_spon_v1",
    clobbers = Abi.desc().clobbers,
    endpoints = { ep("entry"), ep("ok") },
    fact_transfer = fact_transfer(),
  })
  for k, v in pairs(overrides or {}) do f[k] = v end
  f.abi = StencilDesc.lower_to_abi(f)
  return f
end

-- Valid minimal abstract stencil with entry + ok + fact transfer passes and has ABI-lowered numeric metadata.
do
  local f = minimal()
  local ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(f)
  assert_true(ok, table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))
  assert_eq(f.abi.stencil.physical_abi, 1, "ABI physical_abi must be numeric")
  assert_eq(f.abi.endpoints[1].kind, 1, "ABI endpoint kind must be numeric")
  assert_true(tostring(f.abi.stencil.pattern_key):match("^0x%x%x%x%x%x%x%x%x%x%x%x%x%x%x%x%xULL$"), "ABI pattern_key must be a deterministic uint64 literal")
  assert_eq(type(f.abi.stencil.pattern_key_hi), "number", "ABI pattern_key_hi must be numeric")
  assert_eq(type(f.abi.stencil.pattern_key_lo), "number", "ABI pattern_key_lo must be numeric")
  assert_eq(type(f.abi.stencil.selector_sig_hi), "number", "ABI selector_sig_hi must be numeric")
  assert_eq(type(f.abi.stencil.selector_sig_lo), "number", "ABI selector_sig_lo must be numeric")
  assert_true(f.abi.executable == false, "minimal metadata stencil must be abstract/non-executable")
end

-- Missing entry fails.
do
  local ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(minimal({ endpoints = { ep("ok") } }))
  assert_false(ok, "missing entry must fail")
  assert_true(has_error(errors, "entry"), table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))
end

-- Missing endpoint locations fails.
do
  local ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(minimal({ endpoints = { StencilDesc.endpoint({ kind = "entry" }), ep("ok") } }))
  assert_false(ok, "empty endpoint locations must fail")
  assert_true(has_error(errors, "location contract"), table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))
end

-- Missing fact transfer fails.
do
  local f = minimal()
  f.fact_transfer = nil
  local ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(f)
  assert_false(ok, "missing fact_transfer must fail")
  assert_true(has_error(errors, "fact_transfer"), table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))
end

-- Data reloc with role fail or exit fails.
do
  local ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(minimal({ data_relocs = { StencilDesc.data_reloc({ role_kind = "fail", code_offset_kind = "abstract_zero" }) } }))
  assert_false(ok, "fail data reloc must fail")
  assert_true(has_error(errors, "control role"), table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))
  ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(minimal({ data_relocs = { StencilDesc.data_reloc({ role_kind = "exit", code_offset_kind = "abstract_zero" }) } }))
  assert_false(ok, "exit data reloc must fail")
end

-- Non-success endpoint without projection fails.
do
  local f = minimal({ endpoints = { ep("entry"), ep("ok"), ep("guard_exit") } })
  local ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(f)
  assert_false(ok, "guard_exit without projection must fail")
  assert_true(has_error(errors, "without projection"), table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))
end

-- Projection ranges must correspond to actual projection objects.
do
  local f = minimal({ endpoints = { ep("entry"), ep("ok"), ep("guard_exit", { projection_start = 1, n_projections = 1 }) }, projections = {} })
  local ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(f)
  assert_false(ok, "n_projections alone must not validate")
  assert_true(has_error(errors, "out of bounds"), table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))

  f = minimal({ endpoints = { ep("entry"), ep("ok"), ep("guard_exit", { projection_start = 1, n_projections = 1 }) }, projections = { { kind = "BAD", pc = 1 } } })
  ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(f)
  assert_false(ok, "bad projection kind must fail")
  assert_true(has_error(errors, "unknown projection kind"), table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))
end

-- Control reloc using data role fails.
do
  local ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(minimal({ control_relocs = { StencilDesc.control_reloc({ edge_kind = "slot", endpoint_index = 2, code_offset_kind = "abstract_zero" }) } }))
  assert_false(ok, "data role control reloc must fail")
  assert_true(has_error(errors, "data role"), table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))
end

-- Abstract zero code offsets must be marked, so placeholders are explicit.
do
  local ok, errors = StencilDesc.validate_stencil(minimal({ data_relocs = { StencilDesc.data_reloc({ role_kind = "slot", code_offset = 0 }) } }))
  assert_false(ok, "unmarked zero code offset must fail in abstract descriptors")
  assert_true(has_error(errors, "abstract_zero"), table.concat(errors or {}, "\n"))
end

print("ok - SpongeJIT stencil IR validation")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit

package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;" .. package.path

local Facts = require("src.facts")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local Projection = require("src.stencil_projection")
local StencilLower = require("src.stencil_lower")

local function assert_eq(a, b, msg) if a ~= b then error((msg or "assert_eq failed") .. ": " .. tostring(a) .. " ~= " .. tostring(b), 2) end end
local function assert_true(x, msg) if not x then error(msg or "assert_true failed", 2) end end

-- Synced-frame projections are explicit recipes.
do
  local p = Projection.synced_frame({ reason = "guard:is_i64", pc = 7 }, 0)
  assert_eq(p.kind, "SYNCED_FRAME", "projection kind")
  assert_eq(p.pc, 7, "projection pc")
end

-- I64 guard lowering gives every guard exit a projection, preserving source pc and flattened range.
do
  local facts = { Facts.fact("type", Facts.slot("R1"), "is_i64", true) }
  local r = SSA.compile({ { op = "ADDI", a = 1, b = 1, c = 1 } }, facts)
  assert_true(r.ok, table.concat(r.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local fr = StencilLower.generate(r, { facts = facts })
  assert_true(fr.ok, table.concat(fr.errors or {}, "\n"))
  local saw_guard = false
  for _, ep in ipairs(fr.stencil.endpoints or {}) do
    if ep.kind == "guard_exit" then
      saw_guard = true
      assert_true((ep.n_projections or 0) > 0, "guard exit must have projection")
      assert_true(ep.projection_start and fr.stencil.projections[ep.projection_start], "guard projection range missing")
      assert_true(ep.projections and ep.projections[1], "guard projection object missing")
      assert_eq(ep.projections[1].pc, 1, "guard projection must use SSA/stencil source pc")
      assert_eq(fr.stencil.projections[ep.projection_start].pc, 1, "flattened projection must use source pc")
    end
  end
  assert_true(saw_guard, "expected guard_exit endpoint")
end

print("ok - SpongeJIT stencil projection")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit

package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;" .. package.path

local LoopRegions = require("src.loop_regions")
local PucBytecode = require("src.puc_bytecode")

local function assert_eq(a, b, msg)
  if a ~= b then error((msg or "assert_eq failed") .. ": " .. tostring(a) .. " ~= " .. tostring(b), 2) end
end
local function assert_true(x, msg) if not x then error(msg or "assert_true failed", 2) end end

local function row(pc, name, fields)
  local r = { proto = "p", pc = pc, name = name, op = name }
  for k, v in pairs(fields or {}) do r[k] = v end
  return r
end

-- Numeric PUC for topology is recognized as a whole region, not scalar FORPREP/FORLOOP facts.
do
  local rows = {
    row(10, "FORPREP", { a = 2, bx = 3 }),
    row(11, "ADDI", { a = 5, b = 5, c = 1 }),
    row(12, "MOVE", { a = 6, b = 5 }),
    row(13, "RETURN1", { a = 6 }),
    row(14, "FORLOOP", { a = 2, bx = 4 }),
  }
  local regions, rejects = LoopRegions.find_in_rows(rows)
  assert_eq(#rejects, 0, "valid numeric loop rejects")
  assert_eq(#regions, 1, "numeric loop region count")
  local r = regions[1]
  assert_eq(r.kind, "numeric_for_region", "numeric kind")
  assert_eq(r.base, 2, "numeric base")
  assert_eq(r.prep_pc, 10, "numeric prep pc")
  assert_eq(r.body_entry_pc, 11, "numeric body pc")
  assert_eq(r.loop_pc, 14, "numeric loop pc")
  assert_eq(r.exit_pc, 15, "numeric exit pc")
  assert_eq(r.edges.skip.to_pc, 15, "numeric skip edge")
  assert_eq(r.edges.continue_loop.to_pc, 11, "numeric continue edge")
  assert_eq(#r.slot_window, 4, "numeric slot window")
  assert_eq(r.slot_window[1], 2, "numeric first slot")
  assert_eq(r.slot_window[4], 5, "numeric external slot")
end

-- Mismatched loop topology is rejected, so region lowering cannot infer false structure.
do
  local rows = {
    row(1, "FORPREP", { a = 0, bx = 2 }),
    row(4, "FORLOOP", { a = 1, bx = 3 }),
  }
  local regions, rejects = LoopRegions.find_in_rows(rows)
  assert_eq(#regions, 0, "bad numeric region count")
  assert_eq(#rejects, 1, "bad numeric reject count")
  assert_eq(rejects[1].reason, "numeric_for_base_mismatch", "bad numeric reason")
end

-- Generic for topology is its own region class with TFORCALL/yield-facing slots.
do
  local rows = {
    row(20, "TFORPREP", { a = 3, bx = 3 }),
    row(21, "MOVE", { a = 8, b = 8 }),
    row(22, "MOVE", { a = 9, b = 9 }),
    row(23, "RETURN1", { a = 8 }),
    row(24, "TFORCALL", { a = 3, c = 2 }),
    row(25, "TFORLOOP", { a = 3, bx = 5 }),
  }
  local regions, rejects = LoopRegions.find_in_rows(rows)
  assert_eq(#rejects, 0, "valid generic loop rejects")
  assert_eq(#regions, 1, "generic loop region count")
  local r = regions[1]
  assert_eq(r.kind, "generic_for_region", "generic kind")
  assert_eq(r.call_pc, 24, "generic call pc")
  assert_eq(r.loop_pc, 25, "generic loop pc")
  assert_eq(r.result_count, 2, "generic result count")
  assert_eq(r.state_slots.control_slot, 6, "generic control slot")
  assert_eq(r.state_slots.call_func_slot, 6, "generic call function slot")
  assert_eq(r.state_slots.last_result_slot, 8, "generic last result slot")
  assert_true(#r.slot_window >= 6, "generic slot window covers call frame")
end

-- Profile artifacts expose structural loop regions as data for later region lowering.
do
  local rows = {
    row(0, "FORPREP", { a = 0, bx = 1 }),
    row(1, "MOVE", { a = 4, b = 4 }),
    row(2, "FORLOOP", { a = 0, bx = 2 }),
  }
  -- Exercise the public recognizer used by puc_bytecode.profile_files without invoking external tools.
  local regions = LoopRegions.find_in_rows(rows)
  assert_eq(LoopRegions.region_key(regions[1]), "puc_lua_5_5:numeric_for_region:base=0:prep=0:body=1:loop=2:exit=3", "region key")
  assert_true(PucBytecode ~= nil, "puc bytecode module loads loop-region dependency")
end
